title: Product accordion tabs render raw HTML markup — richtext schema rejects <ol>/<li>/<div>
description:
On product pages, the How To Use / Description / Benefits / Ingredients accordion shows raw HTML (
  1. Dispense and emulsify ...
    2. ) as plain text. Root cause: sections/product.liquid and sections/section-product.liquid declare raw_content_1 through raw_content_5 as type:richtext in section schema. Shopify's richtext type rejects
      /
        /
      • /
        and HTML-escapes the entire input when those tags are present. Fix: change schema type from "richtext" to "html" for all raw_content_* fields. Caveat: existing escaped content remains escaped — merchant must re-save affected products in theme editor. If many products affected, follow-up with unescape filter chain as separate fix.

title: Missing blog body images — malformed in article.content
description: Several blog posts (e.g. /blogs/hair-education/2026-best-in-beauty-hair-protocols-essentials — 10 of 11 broken) render tags whose parent tag is missing its closing > before the . Browsers refuse to render images inside malformed anchor wraps. Source HTML in article.content is already malformed (rich-text editor or vendor app produced bad HTML). The article-content-with-img-dims.liquid post-processor preserves the malformation. Fix: add defensive replace chain at top of post-processor to repair target="_blank"

title: Liquid syntax error on branded collection pages — quote-style mismatch in title-strip replace chain
description: Live-site Liquid syntax error (Unexpected character é) renders verbatim on /collections/kerastase, /collections/kerastase-genesis, /collections/kerastase-chroma-absolu. Root cause: replace: filter chain in snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid:187 and snippets/product-grid-item.liquid:294 mixes single + double quotes; entries 3 and 4 use single quotes around strings containing apostrophe (L'Oréal) which terminates the Liquid string literal early. Fix: standardize all 14 entries to double quotes. Bug provenance: introduced 2026-04-29 commit 0614791 (ujh) and copy-pasted into branded variant on 2026-05-03 commit cb485b9 (aoe consolidation).

title: Phase 2 CLS: investigate #MainContent late-injection sources (read-only)
description:
Parent: hairmnl-theme-987 (desktop CLS p75 0.20, 44% good)

GA4 RUM data shows ~160 CLS events where debug_target resolves to #MainContent or immediate children. These are the largest unaddressed CLS bucket on desktop.

### Goal
INVESTIGATION ONLY. No code fixes in this issue. Map which widgets/scripts inject content into #MainContent after initial render, causing layout shift.

### Investigation targets
1. LimeSpot recommendation strips (homepage, PDP, cart) — do they inject via JS after DOMContentLoaded?
2. Vertex AI rec rails — if any are wired to homepage or PDP, when do they insert?
3. Klaviyo embedded forms — any inside #MainContent that expand after JS hydration?
4. LoyaltyLion widgets inside page body (not just launcher)
5. Smart SEO / Judge.me review widgets that expand below product description
6. Reamaze chat widget if positioned inside #MainContent (unlikely but check)
7. Any Shopify app blocks (theme.liquid section-group) that have lazy JS init

### Output required (write to bd notes via bd update --append-notes)
- List each widget/source found, with:
  - CSS selector of the injected element
  - Approximate timing (before/after DOMContentLoaded, before/after load event)
  - Estimated CLS contribution (high/medium/low) based on element size + position
  - Recommended fix approach (reserve space via min-height, content-visibility, font-display:swap, etc.)
- Rank top 3 candidates for Phase 2 follow-up fix batches

### Files to read (no edits)
- layout/theme.liquid (section groups, app blocks)
- sections/featured-collection.liquid (LimeSpot integration points)
- snippets/ — any snippet that uses {{ app.block }} or JS app init
- assets/custom-theme.js — look for DOMContentLoaded / load handlers that inject content
- assets/theme.js (if present) — same scan

### Hard constraints
- READ ONLY. Do NOT write any code changes.
- Do NOT run shopify theme push.
- Do NOT close 987 (parent issue stays open until all Phase 2 fixes land).
notes:
Investigation-only dispatch. Cursor overnight chain 2026-05-12.
## hairmnl-theme-odv — #MainContent late-injection investigation (read-only)

### DOM boundary
- `#MainContent` = `<main class="main-content" id="MainContent">` in `layout/theme.liquid` (~779). Children: `{{ content_for_layout }}` (all route sections), then `<noscript>`, then `<limespot></limespot>` (unless `customers/*`), then BSS snippet renders — all still **inside** `<main>`.
- `{% section 'footer' %}`, `{% section 'popups' %}`, search popdown, `[data-modal-container]`, deferred `custom-theme.js`, Klaviyo listener, `{% render 'mbc-bundles' %}` are **after** `</main>` — GA4 `debug_target` on `#MainContent` usually means shifts attributed to an ancestor of the shifted node; some overlays attach under `body` but shift in-flow siblings still attributed upstream.

### 1) LimeSpot (Personalizer.io)
- **Selectors:** `<limespot></limespot>` (custom element); runtime DOM per theme docs / preview CSS: `[id^="LP_"]`, `[class*="LP_Inline"]`, `[class*="LP_GridContainer"]`, `[class*="LP_RecommendationsBlock"]`, `[class*="LimeSpot"]`, `[data-personalizer-fragment]`.
- **Timing:** `snippets/limespot.liquid` sets `window.LimeSpot` + `PageInfo` inline in `<head>`; SDK `edge.personalizer.io/.../storefront.min.js` loaded with **defer** (via app pipeline / theme). Population of `<limespot>` occurs **after** HTML parse when deferred script runs — effectively **after DOMContentLoaded ordering relative to parser**, often before `load` depending on network.
- **CLS estimate:** **Medium** (outer slot partially mitigated: `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` documents 0×0 inline unknown element → ~400px growth shifting footer; `limespot { display:block; contain:layout }` reduces **sibling** shift). **Residual:** internal Flickity/Swiper init can still reflow **inside** the slot (contained).
- **Fix approach:** Keep min-height/skeleton for worst-case rail height where needed; ensure images in injected cards have width/height; avoid adding new containing-block props on ancestors of fixed overlays (kt0 rule).

### 2) Vertex AI rec rails
- **Selectors:** `.vertex-rec-slot`, `[data-vertex-placement]`, `.vertex-buy-it-again` (when enabled).
- **Timing:** **Server-rendered Liquid** via `snippets/vertex-recommendations.liquid` / `vertex-buy-it-again.liquid` in sections (`sections/product.liquid`, `sections/cart.liquid`, `sections/section-banner-slider.liquid`, etc.). On **main** theme these renders are **commented out** (“Temporarily disabled on main until vertex snippet ships”).
- **CLS estimate:** **Low** when enabled (SSR HTML present at first paint); **N/A** on current main if stays disabled. Image lazy-load without dimensions could add **low–medium** shifts.
- **Fix approach:** Explicit image dimensions on cards; empty-state min-height when metafields nil.

### 3) Klaviyo
- **Selectors:** `body > [class*="klaviyo-form"]`, `body > [class*="kl-private-reset-css"]`, `body > [data-testid^="klaviyo-form"]` per `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` (e4a-γ) — **body-level** modal from App Embed; theme asserts **zero** `klaviyo-form` embeds in `sections/`/`snippets/`.
- **Timing:** After Klaviyo loader + form open — typically **post-DOMContentLoaded**.
- **CLS estimate:** **Low** for `#MainContent`-scoped shifts if forms stay body-only; **medium** if a future embed is placed in main (would push PDP content — PSI note in e4a-γ).
- **Fix approach:** Keep forms body-level; reserve overlay dimmer space; `font-display` for any Klaviyo webfonts if CLS ties to text.

### 4) LoyaltyLion
- **Selectors:** `#loyaltylion` (app embed root); `.lion-reward-item`, `.lion-reward-item__icon` on **pages/rewards**; header `.points-box` / `data-lion-points` (header is **outside** `#MainContent`).
- **Timing:** `snippets/loyaltylion.liquid` in `<head>`; rewards page `sections/page.liquid` uses **DOMContentLoaded** + `setInterval` polling until `#loyaltylion` exists, then mutates reward icons — **after** LL injects its widget into page body (inside `#MainContent` for that template).
- **CLS estimate:** **Medium** on `/pages/rewards` for icon swaps; **low** elsewhere unless in-body widgets added. Body notifications addressed separately (e4a-δ).
- **Fix approach:** Reserve card grid min-heights; replace polling with MutationObserver + single pass; static dimensions for reward icons.

### 5) Judge.me / Smart SEO
- **Judge.me selectors:** `#judgeme_product_reviews`, `.jdgm-widget.jdgm-review-widget`, `.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge`, `.jdgm-medals-wrapper`, `.jdgm-carousel-wrapper`, etc. (`snippets/judgeme_widgets.liquid`). Core `jdgm-settings-script` + `jdgm-script` via **`{{ content_for_header }}`** (theme comment ~726–732).
- **Timing:** Metafield HTML in Liquid first paint; **Judge.me JS** hydrates/expands (documented `.jdgm-hidden` → visible pattern in css-overrides). **After** deferred Judge scripts execute — typically **after DOMContentLoaded**.
- **Smart SEO:** Mostly **commented** meta wrappers in `layout/theme.liquid` / sections; `snippets/pluginseo-structured-data.liquid` is JSON-LD — **not** a late layout injector into `#MainContent`.
- **CLS estimate:** **High** for PDP `#judgeme_product_reviews` + preview badge row (large block below description); **medium** for grid `judgeme_preview_badge` on collection cards.
- **Fix approach:** Continue static stars + min-height patterns; `content-visibility` for below-fold review block; ensure widget container min-height matches hydrated content.

### 6) Reamaze
- **Selectors:** `#reamaze-widget-label`, `[id^="reamaze-widget"]` (css-overrides 7fz).
- **Timing:** Third-party embed after CDN script — **post-DOMContentLoaded**.
- **CLS estimate:** **Low–medium** (theme forces `position:fixed` + `contain:layout` on label/iframe hosts to stop flow participation). Unlikely “inside #MainContent” DOM placement; may still appear in GA4 as ancestor attribution.
- **Fix approach:** Keep fixed positioning; monitor if embed changes DOM structure.

### 7) Shopify app blocks + `content_for_header`
- **`{% when '@app' %}{% render block %}`** in `sections/product.liquid` (~129–130): any Online Store 2.0-style **app block** configured in Theme Editor can inject arbitrary HTML/JS **inside** the product column within `#MainContent` — **high variance**.
- **`{{ content_for_header }}`:** Injects app scripts (Judge.me, GTM, etc.). Late DOM is app-specific.
- **CLS estimate:** **High (unbounded)** for unknown app blocks; **medium** for known globals.
- **Fix approach:** Audit Theme Editor app blocks per template; reserve space per app docs; defer noncritical apps.

### 8) Other theme-identified injectors touching main flow
- **Zapiet Store Pickup:** `.product__pickup [data-store-availability-container]` — JS fills empty div (`theme.dev.js` pickupContainer). **Timing:** after deferred `storepickup.js`. **CLS:** mitigated with `min-height: 64px` + `contain:layout` on `.product__pickup` (e4a-α).
- **BOGOS / free gifts:** `#freegifts-main-page-container` in `sections/cart.liquid` (~35) inside main cart flow — **JS populates**. **CLS:** css-overrides BOGOS section (3uw/c8k) targets `#freegifts-main-popup-container`, `#sca-fg-notifications`. **Medium** on cart if inline glider shifts content.
- **Searchanise:** `//www.searchanise.com/widgets/shopify/init.js` **defer** in `layout/theme.liquid` (~172). Can enhance collection/search DOM — **medium** on listing pages inside main.
- **Bold Brain / MBC:** `{% render 'mbc-bundles' %}` after main — cart/bundle UI may still affect layout when interacting; mostly outside `#MainContent`.
- **custom-theme.js:** Multiple `DOMContentLoaded` handlers (e.g. `.cross-post-blogs` Swiper via IntersectionObserver ~8438+, `.metafield-multi_line_text_field` textContent strip ~8948+, cart consent modal ~8962+). **cross-post-blogs** lives in article template inside main — init on intersection can **resize** carousel — **low–medium** when scrolled into view.
- **theme.js / theme.dev.js:** Only `.main-content` usage found is **transparent header** feature detection selectors (~5314), not content injection.

### File scope note
- **`sections/featured-collection.liquid`:** **not present** in this repo (Pipeline uses other section names, e.g. collection templates). Investigation used `sections/collection.liquid`, `sections/product.liquid`, `layout/theme.liquid`, `snippets/*`, `assets/custom-theme.js` per bd.

---

### Top 3 Phase 2 follow-up fix batches (ranked)
1. **Judge.me** — PDP `#judgeme_product_reviews` + `.jdgm-prev-badge` / collection preview badges: largest predictable vertical hydration inside `#MainContent`; existing CLS fixes prove ongoing risk.
2. **LimeSpot** — `<limespot>` + runtime `LP_*` / `LimeSpot` grids on home, collection, PDP, cart: deferred SDK + rich carousel DOM; outer contain done, internal reflow + image metrics still fair game.
3. **Cart BOGOS + Searchanise** (tie-break batch) — `#freegifts-main-page-container` + Searchanise-enhanced collection DOM: route-specific late fill; second-order after Judge+LimeSpot unless GA4 shows cart/collection dominates.

---

title: PDP CLS+INP — Judge.me preview badge reservation + gallery zoom yield (884 URLs)
description:
GSC mobile CWV report (5/10) shows 884 of 1,231 CLS-failing URLs (72%) are query-param variants of /products/kerastase-blond-absolu-purple-shampoo-250ml. Same product also accounts for 884/884 (100%) of the INP > 200ms cohort. RUM CWV dashboard top_inp_targets confirms: PDP image gallery (flickity-slider > product__media > lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper > img.srcset) is the #2 INP target site-wide.

Two distinct fixes, both PDP-wide (benefit every product page with reviews + every product page period):

## CLS — Judge.me preview badge space reservation (top of PDP)

The product page renders <div class='jdgm-widget jdgm-prev-badge' data-id='...' data-number-of-reviews='21' data-average-rating='4.95'> just below the product title. The div is initially EMPTY in server HTML — Judge.me's loader.js hydrates it with stars + "21 reviews" text post-load.

Without space reservation, the badge grows from 0 → ~24px tall, pushing the price, variant selector, ATC button, and entire below-fold content down. This is a TOP-OF-PAGE shift, which CLS scores heavily because it shifts a large area of the viewport.

Existing CSS only covers bottom-of-PDP medals wrapper (snippets/css-overrides.liquid:168). The top-of-page snippet has no reservation.

## INP — PDP image gallery zoom handler

The flickity main gallery's mobile-zoom-wrapper is responsible for 27 INP events in the last 7d (target: div.flickity-slider>div.product__media.is-selected>div>div.lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper>img.srcset). When user taps to zoom, the synchronous tap-handler does too much work (init zoom-pswp lightbox, load high-res image, animate). Goal: bring single-tap p75 INP under 200ms.
design:
## Phase A — CLS fix (low-risk, high-leverage)

Add to snippets/css-overrides.liquid (after the existing jdgm-medals-wrapper rule at line 168):

```css
/* CLS fix YYYY-MM-DD <bd-id> — Judge.me preview badge top-of-PDP shift.
   <div class="jdgm-widget jdgm-prev-badge"> renders empty at PDP top
   below product title; loader.js injects stars + "N reviews" → grows
   from 0 → ~24px → top-of-page shift → 884 URLs failing CLS on
   kerastase-blond-absolu PDP grouping. Reserve typical badge height
   so the hydration is paint-only, not layout. contain:layout
   isolates internal star-count changes from cascading. */
.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge {
  display: inline-block;
  min-height: 24px;
  min-width: 100px;
  contain: layout;
}
```

Files: snippets/css-overrides.liquid (CSS-only, ~10 lines).

Verification: 24h post-deploy, GSC validation should rerun and the 884 affected URLs should drop out of CLS-failing cohort.

## Phase B — INP fix (more invasive)

Investigation needed:
1. Identify which JS handler is the synchronous slow path on mobile-zoom-wrapper tap. Likely candidates:
   - flickity click → cellChange recompute (heavy if many slides)
   - zoom-pswp PhotoSwipe init on first tap (lazy, 1-time cost)
   - mobile-zoom-wrapper handler in custom-theme.js (no sourcemaps — deferred per 3sr)
2. Strategies:
   - Defer PhotoSwipe library import until idle (currently loaded eagerly?)
   - Yield with requestIdleCallback inside the tap handler so the layout/paint can occur within INP budget
   - Use scheduler.postTask if available (not all browsers)
3. Validation: synthetic Lighthouse PWA audit + real INP rerun in 24h.

Files: assets/zoom-pswp.js (or wherever zoom is initialized), assets/theme.dev.js + assets/theme.js (mirror discipline per .opencode_hints).

Phase A unblocks GSC quickly. Phase B is a separate dispatch.
acceptance_criteria:
## Phase A acceptance
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid contains a `.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge` rule with min-height/min-width/contain:layout, following the existing CLS fix comment convention (date + bd-id + reason).
- Diff is CSS-only, no Liquid or JS changes.
- Pushed to draft, then live (smoke: visit kerastase PDP on mobile incognito, badge area pre-reserved before Judge.me hydrates).
- 24h post-deploy: GSC validation rerun on the CLS-mobile-0.1 issue should show the 884 kerastase-blond-absolu URLs drop out (or shift to needs-improvement → good).

## Phase B acceptance (separate dispatch)
- Mobile gallery first-tap INP p75 < 200ms (measured via web-vitals reporter, debug_target containing mobile-zoom-wrapper).
- No regression in zoom UX (lightbox still opens, images still high-res).
- assets/theme.js minified mirror updated to match assets/theme.dev.js source change per .opencode_hints mirror discipline.

title: RUM dashboard LCP regression is GTM instrumentation, not perf — diff V141 vs V140 + fix
description:
RUM CWV dashboard shows LCP-good dropped from 84% (May 7) to 66% (May 10) — but this is an INSTRUMENTATION regression, not a real perf regression.

Evidence:
- N halved between May 7 → May 8: LCP 25,497→12,046 (-53%); CLS -46%; FCP -60%; TTFB -33%; INP -1% (FLAT).
- Only INP (interaction-fired) preserved volume; the 4 load-time metrics dropped 33-60%.
- top_templates.LCP shows 95.7% of events have template="(not set)" — custom dimension parameter is no longer being captured.
- Dashboard query at scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:384 silently drops events with metric_rating="(not set)" by only counting good/needs-improvement/poor — masks the real issue.
- Timing aligns exactly with c03ab92 (l3g GTM fix, Container Version 141 published May 6-7).

Hypothesis: GTM Container Version 141 republish inadvertently affected the web-vitals GA4 tag(s) — likely either trigger config or eventSettingsTable param mappings. Need to diff V141 vs V140 to confirm.
design:
## Investigation + fix plan

### Phase 1 — Diagnosis (read-only via GTM API)
1. List all GTM container versions; locate V140 (pre-fix) and V141 (post-fix l3g publish).
2. Diff V141 vs V140: enumerate every tag/trigger/variable that changed. Should be exactly tag 776 + DLVs 773/774/775 if the l3g fix was clean.
3. Locate the web-vitals tags (firing on Custom Event = LCP|CLS|INP|FCP|TTFB). Inspect their firing triggers, eventSettingsTable parameter mappings (especially `metric_rating` and `template`), and tag IDs.
4. Confirm whether the metric_rating + template params reach GA4 via DebugView or via the V141 export inspection.

### Phase 2 — Fix in fresh workspace
- Create a NEW workspace (NOT 197 or 190 or any leftover) from current live (V141).
- For each broken web-vitals tag:
  - Restore the broken parameter mappings to their V140 form.
- Create + publish a new Container Version. Coordinator self-publishes since the user authorized autonomous fix.

### Phase 3 — Defensive dashboard fix
- scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:384 — count events with metric_rating="(not set)" into a separate "unknown" bucket (or surface count alongside totals) so silent param failures are visible going forward.

### Phase 4 — Verify
- Wait 24h; rerun dashboard. LCP-good should rebound to ~84%; template column should populate.
- After 7d, legacy "(not set)" rows age out of the rolling window.

## Out of scope
- Re-publishing the l3g fix (it was intentional and correct — JS error tag still needs trigger 772).
- Further changes to the 3 orphan-trigger tags (4r8, t4m, miy) — separate bd issues.
acceptance_criteria:
- GTM Container Version diff (V141 vs V140) captured in bd notes; smoking gun confirmed.
- Web-vitals tag(s) restored to working state via new container version published from a fresh workspace.
- Dashboard query at line 384 updated to count or surface (not set) ratings (defensive).
- 24h post-fix dashboard refresh shows LCP-good >= 80% and template column populated with real Liquid template names (index, product, blog, etc.).
- bd issue closed with phantom-completion guard (git show HEAD --stat in notes).

title: /cart INP regression — gjv fix did not move needle (41% poor 4d post-fix vs 30% pre-fix)
description: gjv (commit 3a22d94, 2026-05-05) scoped cart--loading class to active row to fix style-recalc. Field data 4d post-fix shows /cart INP poor% WORSE: May 4 30% → May 9 41%. Hypothesis: third-party widget orchestration (LimeSpot, Klaviyo, BOGOS, Reamaze, sticky-bar) is the dominant cost, not the style-recalc gjv addressed. Need DevTools investigation on /cart with line items + qty stepper interaction.
design:
## Goal
Diagnose why /cart INP is worse 4 days post-gjv (41% poor) than the May 4 pre-fix baseline (30% poor). gjv (commit 3a22d94, 2026-05-05) scoped `cart--loading` class to active row instead of page wrapper — should have dropped style-recalc from O(N) to O(1) on each stepper click. Field data shows the fix did NOT move the needle.

## Hypothesis
Third-party widget orchestration on /cart is the dominant INP cost, not our cart-stepper handler:
- LimeSpot SDK (gated 2026-04-28 but cart still has widget mounts)
- Klaviyo signup popup (deferred 20 sec + 60% scroll, but still hydrates)
- BOGOS free-gift recompute on cart change (see bd "bogos-error" for separate bug)
- Sticky add-to-cart bar
- Reamaze widget hydration

The gjv fix addressed the WRONG thing — it fixed style-recalc from a Liquid-level class addition, but the real INP cost is JS ORCHESTRATION across these widgets when cart state changes.

## Investigation steps (Brave + Claude-in-Chrome MCP, mobile emulation)
1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/cart with at least 2 line items in mobile emulation (Moto G Power, Slow 4G).
2. DevTools → Performance → Record. Click qty stepper. Stop recording.
3. In the timeline, identify the LONG TASK that immediately follows the click event.
4. Expand the long task: which JS frame dominates? Which third-party origin?
5. Cross-check with GA4 RUM `debug_target` for /cart — look for the most-attributed selectors beyond `#shopify-section-template--XXX__main` (which is the generic cart body wrapper).
6. Repeat for: drawer open, line-item remove, freegift add.

## Likely fixes (ranked by leverage)
- A. Defer BOGOS recompute to requestIdleCallback (don't block first paint after cart change)
- B. Debounce qty stepper clicks (250ms — most users are spam-clicking)
- C. Yield to main thread between cart-update tasks (`scheduler.postTask()` or setTimeout 0)
- D. Disable widget re-init on cart state change (rely on the widget's own state hydration)

## Files likely to modify (post-investigation)
- assets/theme.dev.js + theme.js — CartItems class, drawer logic
- snippets/* — third-party widget includes
- Possibly snippets/limespot.liquid, snippets/klaviyo-signup-script.liquid, BOGOS-related snippets

## Out of scope
- Reverting the gjv fix (the row-scoping is still correct; just insufficient)
- Major refactor of cart UX
- Vertex cutover (separate, gated on Decision 2)

## Wait for clean signal
The full 7-day RUM window post-fix closes 2026-05-12. Investigate via DevTools NOW (synthetic) and confirm the directional signal with the May 12+ data.
acceptance_criteria:
After investigation + targeted fix:
- /cart INP poor% drops from 41% → ≤15% (matches site-wide 81% INP good)
- CrUX mobile INP p75 holds at ≤251ms or improves
- No regression on cart functionality (qty steppers, drawer, line-item remove all work)
- Specific dominant blocker (per debug_target) documented in commit message + bd notes
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-05-10 — root cause identified.

## /cart INP attribution (last 14d, GA4 Data API)
37/41 (90%) of poor-INP events on /cart target the SAME selector chain:
  #shopify-section-template--XXX__main > div.cart__template > div.wrapper.pt2.pb4

The two different template--XXX values are just different Shopify section instances. The browser couldn't isolate a more specific child target — meaning the slow handler is bound far up the DOM tree (event delegation OR full-wrapper re-render).

## Static analysis — CartItems.updateCart() flow (assets/theme.dev.js:4116)

When user clicks a qty stepper, the click→paint critical path includes ALL of:

1. `cart.js` GET (read current state)               ← network 1
2. `cart/change.js` POST (mutate)                   ← network 2
3. `theme:cart:change` event dispatch — TWO other listeners run (line 1716 = header cart count badge, line 2572 = drawer state sync)
4. `?section_id=api-cart-items` GET (re-render HTML)  ← network 3
5. `this.items.innerHTML = …` swap (full DOM re-creation)
6. `initQuantity()` re-binds ALL input + remove handlers
7. `initUpsell()` runs ProductAddButton constructor
8. `?section_id=api-cart-subtotal` GET (re-render subtotal) ← network 4
9. `this.subtotal.innerHTML = …` swap

**4 sequential network round-trips + 2 innerHTML swaps + 3-5 listener cascades, all on the click→paint critical path.** That's the INP cost.

The gjv fix (commit 3a22d94) optimized step 6's per-row class addition (O(N) → O(1)) — a real but TINY contribution to the total chain. It correctly fixed what it targeted but didn't address the dominant cost.

## Recommended Phase 2 fix (bounded, ~50 LoC)

Replace 4 sequential fetches with 1 using Shopify's bulk-sections cart API:

```
POST /cart/change.js
Body: { id, quantity, sections: "api-cart-items,api-cart-subtotal", sections_url: "/cart" }
Response: { ...cart, sections: { "api-cart-items": "<html>", "api-cart-subtotal": "<html>" } }
```

Eliminates: 1 GET (cart.js prefetch), 1 GET (api-cart-items), 1 GET (api-cart-subtotal). Keeps: 1 POST. Returns the new cart state AND both rendered section HTML in one round-trip.

**Files:**
- assets/theme.dev.js — `updateCart` method (~lines 4116-4169) + `loadForm`/`updateTotal`
- assets/theme.js — minified mirror (find by string `cart/change.js`)

**Expected impact (provisional):**
- /cart INP poor% drops from 41% → <20% (matches site-wide ~19% poor)
- Removes ~3 round-trips of network latency from the critical path
- Stacks additively with the BOGOS empty-cart gate shipped 2026-05-10 (commit c745875)

## Wait to dispatch

The BOGOS gate shipped today (commit c745875). Its effect on /cart INP won't be measurable until ~2026-05-17 (full 7-day post-fix RUM window). Recommend:
1. Wait until 2026-05-13 (3 days post-BOGOS-gate) — enough to see directional signal on whether BOGOS was material
2. If /cart INP poor% is still ≥30%, dispatch the bulk-sections-API fix
3. If it dropped significantly (e.g. <20%), close o0z and let the BOGOS gate do its work

## Other potential follow-ups (defer)
- Debounce qty stepper for rapid clicks (~10 LoC, in initInputs)
- Defer `theme:cart:change` listeners to requestIdleCallback (~5 LoC)
- Avoid full innerHTML swap (only update changed quantity field) — too invasive for now

description: Top 2 friction pages by LCP poor% are blog posts on /blogs/hair-education/. Article hero preload already in place (9hw). LCP element must be something else — likely the first in-body image, not the hero. Need DevTools investigation in Brave to confirm, then ship a targeted preload/fetchpriority extension.
design:
## Goal
Identify the actual LCP element on the two top friction blog posts and ship a targeted fix. Article hero preload (issue 9hw, 2026-05-04) is already in place — so the LCP element on these blog posts is something OTHER than the hero.

## Affected pages (from GA4 RUM 7d, latest snapshot 2026-05-09)
- /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-the-transformative-power-of-purple-shampoo (49 poor / 77 total = 63% poor LCP)
- /blogs/hair-education/rinse-out-conditioner-vs-leave-in-conditioner-what-s-the-difference (41 poor / 88 total = 47% poor)

## Investigation steps (Brave + Claude-in-Chrome MCP, mobile emulation)
1. Open each URL in Brave with mobile (Moto G Power, Slow 4G) emulation.
2. Open DevTools → Performance → Record a fresh page load.
3. Identify the LCP candidate from the timeline (highlight the LCP marker → element).
4. Hypothesis to test: largest body image is the LCP, NOT the hero. Article images are 480x480 from the post-processor — likely too small for hero-replacement role on mobile.
5. Cross-check with GA4 RUM event params (event=largest_contentful_paint, debug_target field).

## Likely fixes (ranked by leverage, choose after investigation)
- A. Inject fetchpriority="high" + preload on the FIRST in-body image (not just the article.image hero) — extends 9hw pattern.
- B. Lazy-load below-fold body images more aggressively (current: native loading=lazy; could add IntersectionObserver-driven decode hints).
- C. If LCP is text-on-cover (overlay over hero), reserve aspect-ratio earlier.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid (extend post-processor to inject fetchpriority on first image)
- sections/article.liquid (template-level preload if first body image's URL can be derived server-side)

## NOT dispatchable
This requires DevTools-based investigation which OpenCode (headless) cannot perform. Keep on CC + browser MCP.

notes:

title: GTM tag 415 (Klaviyo Form Submission Listener) references orphan trigger 2147479553 — email automation revenue tracking broken

Without a valid trigger, the tag cannot fire — email automation revenue attribution is broken (won't credit emails for purchases triggered after form submissions). notes:
DIAGNOSIS UPDATE 2026-05-10T10:50:17Z — no GTM fix needed; misdiagnosis in original bd.

Trigger ID 2147479553 is GTM's BUILT-IN 'All Pages' trigger. Built-in triggers don't appear in workspaces().triggers().list() (why prior session flagged it as orphan) but ARE compiled into the live container.

Evidence:
1. Live gtm.js?id=GTM-M4NKSBD shows tag 415 compiled with predicate 1 = '_eq macro=0 arg1=gtm.js' — fires on every page load.
2. Klaviyo's GTM setup guide (Analytics Mania klaviyo-forms recipe; developers.klaviyo.com): 'Set this tag to fire on all pages.' Listener tags must attach early on every page.
3. Tag HTML pushes dl_subscribe to dataLayer on submit; downstream trigger 409 (Event - Klaviyo Email Form Submit) fires Klaviyo tags. Chain intact.

If GA4/Klaviyo email-attribution numbers look wrong in reports, the root cause is NOT this tag's trigger. File a fresh bd with the GA4 data showing the gap.

title: Fix GTM JS Error tag - wrong trigger + event name mismatch
description:
The GA4 - JS Error Event tag (GTM-M4NKSBD, live version 140) is misconfigured, causing GA4 to show (not set) for all error_type/error_message/error_source dimensions despite 40K+ js_error events.

**Root cause (confirmed by live GTM inspection, 2026-05-06):**
- Tag fires on: "Initialization - All Pages" (fires at page load, not on errors)
- Event name sent to GA4: "js_error" (old name)
- Theme pushes: "hairmnl_js_error" with fields error_type/error_message/error_source

**Result:** tag fires on every page load with empty dimension values, NOT on actual JS errors. The real error events (hairmnl_js_error) never reach the tag because the trigger is wrong.

**Also found:** 3 other critical tags have orphaned trigger ID 2147479553 (Conversion Linker, Klaviyo Form Listener, TrafficGuard) — these also need investigation.

**Fix required (GTM, not theme):**
1. Change GA4 - JS Error Event trigger from "Initialization - All Pages" → "Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error" (trigger 772)
2. Verify eventName is correct in tag parameters
3. Fix the 3 other broken tags (2147479553) 
4. Publish to live
5. Update dashboard query to also capture "hairmnl_js_error" events

**Dashboard also needs updating** (scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py): the JS error query filters eventName=js_error, but theme now sends hairminl_js_error. Need _in filter for both event names.

**Files involved:**
- scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py (dashboard query update)
- layout/theme.liquid (already correct - do not change)
- GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD (workspace 197) - fix via GTM UI or API

**Context:** theme was migrated 2026-05-02 (commit 5fb4be7) to hairmnl_js_error naming to avoid collision with Elevar's js_error. GTM trigger should have been updated at that time.
notes:
Handoff file: docs/gtm-js-error-handoff-2026-05-06.md

GTM fixes needed (live container v140, all HIGH priority):

1. GA4 - JS Error Event (tag 776): Change trigger from 'Initialization - All Pages' → 'Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error' (trigger 772). Keep eventName=js_error. Publish.

2. Conversion Linker (tag 12): Broken trigger 2147479553. Fix by reconnecting to valid trigger. Impact: all GA4 conversion attribution broken.

3. Klaviyo - Form Submission Listener (tag 415): Broken trigger 2147479553. Fix by reconnecting to valid trigger. Impact: email automation flows broken.

4. TrafficGuard Tag (tag 94): Broken trigger 2147479553. Fix by reconnecting to valid trigger. Impact: fraud detection disabled.

All 4 tags share the same non-existent trigger ID — likely a deleted custom event trigger. Fix all in same GTM session, then publish container.

Dashboard update: scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py line ~503 — change _eq('eventName', 'js_error') to _in('eventName', ['js_error', 'hairmnl_js_error']).

title: Dashboard: bump GHA --psi-runs from 2 to 3
description:
The perf-dashboard GHA workflow passes --psi-runs 2 to build-perf-dashboard.py. The script's run_psi_median() runs PSI n times and returns the median. With n=2, median([a,b]) = (a+b)/2 (an average, not a true median). With n=3, the middle value is a proper median that rejects a single outlier run. The script default is already 3; the workflow override of 2 is below the useful minimum.

One-line fix in .github/workflows/perf-dashboard.yml.

title: Dashboard: show daily PSI median + noise band instead of latest-run value
description:
The PSI lab trend charts (Score, LCP, TBT, CLS) show the LATEST PSI run of each day. When one bad late-night Lighthouse run fires (e.g. network jitter, cold CDN cache, one slow third-party), it replaces the earlier good values and the chart looks like a regression even though real-user CrUX is flat.

Root cause confirmed 2026-05-12: May 11 had a 23:51Z run with LCP=32s/CLS=0.65/score=11, immediately followed at 00:02Z by score=32/LCP=8s/CLS=0.02 — same page, same code, 10 min apart. The 23:51Z run became the day's data point because _merge_daily_snapshot keeps the latest PSI timestamp.

Fix: accumulate all PSI runs per day, expose median + min/max so the chart shows a noise band rather than a noisy line.

title: Phase 2 CLS: LimeSpot card image dimension reservation
description:
Parent: hairmnl-theme-987 (desktop CLS p75 0.20, 44% good)

odv investigation: LimeSpot (Personalizer.io) injects LP_* / LimeSpot* grid cards after deferred SDK loads. Outer slot containment is already in place (limespot { display:block; contain:layout } in css-overrides.liquid). The residual CLS comes from internal image reflow — injected product images lack explicit width/height attributes, so the browser doesn't know their aspect ratio before they load.

Fix: target the product images inside LimeSpot-injected containers and reserve their aspect ratio via CSS.

### Files to modify
- assets/custom-theme.css (append only)

### What to add
```css
/* ── CLS fix 2026-05-12 hairmnl-theme-BDID (987 Phase 2 — LimeSpot card image dims) ── */
[class*='LP_GridContainer'] img,
[class*='LP_Inline'] img,
[class*='LP_RecommendationsBlock'] img,
[data-personalizer-fragment] img {
  aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;
  width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  object-fit: cover;
}
```

These selectors target only LimeSpot-injected card images (runtime DOM). Standard pipeline product images have their own aspect-ratio rules and are not matched by these selectors.

Note: aspect-ratio 1/1 matches HairMNL product image format (square shots). If LimeSpot renders non-square images, adjust to 3/4 or 4/3.

### Hard constraints
- These selectors are inherently LimeSpot-scoped (LP_* classes are unique to LimeSpot SDK). No body.template-* prefix needed, but do NOT remove it if adding one.
- NO contain/transform/filter/will-change on these selectors (kt0 rule — LimeSpot carousel slides may have fixed/absolute overlays).
- CSS only. No JS.
- Only stage assets/custom-theme.css.
- No shopify push, no git push, no bd close.

title: 987 Phase 2 article-cards: redo with template-body scope (Option B)
description:
First attempt (commit 3b9cf26, reverted 2026-05-12 in commit 279b2c9) broke /blogs/hair-education and any page using .homepage-blog class because the new CSS (display:flex on .grid--uniform + float:none on .grid__item) collided with Pipeline 6's float-based width-class grid (.one-third / .medium-up--one-third etc.). Flex items lost their width and stacked vertically full-width instead of laying out 3-up.

Root cause learned: Pipeline 6 reuses .homepage-blog beyond the literal homepage (blog index templates share the section). Scoping by class wasn't enough — needed to also preserve the width-class math.

Three viable re-approaches:

Option A — flex-basis mirroring float widths: Add flex-basis calc(100%/3) (and breakpoint variants) to grid items so flex layout matches the float layout's width semantics.

Option B — template-body-scope: Use selector body.template-index .homepage-blog .grid.grid--uniform so the rule only fires on the literal homepage. Doesn't affect blog index templates.

Option C — keep JS, accept the small cost: .homepage-blog is below-fold on homepage (IntersectionObserver already gates the JS). The actual CLS contribution may be small in practice — 78 poor events / 7d is modest vs #MainContent (160) and cart template (40). Acceptable to skip article-cards and prioritize higher-impact contributors.

Reference: bd 987 notes contain full Phase 1 audit + regression root cause. Reference commits: 3b9cf26 (broken), 279b2c9 (revert).
design:
Recommended path: Option B (template-body-scope).

Reasoning:
- Cleanest scope: only fires where the homepage actually uses the section
- Doesn't have to math-match float widths (Pipeline 6 grid breakpoints are non-trivial)
- Blog index templates continue working as-is

Concrete change:
1. Restore the same JS removal (delete the IIFE at the original location in custom-theme.js).
2. Add CSS to custom-theme.css scoped by body.template-index:
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .grid.grid--uniform: display flex, flex-wrap wrap
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .grid__item: display flex, flex-direction column, float none
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .article__card.section--details: display flex, flex-direction column, flex 1 1 auto
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .article__card.section--details .title: flex 1 1 auto

title: Investigate + fix desktop CLS p75 stuck at 0.20 (44% good)
description:
Desktop CLS p75 is 0.20 in CrUX 28-day data (window ending 2026-05-09). Distribution: 44.0% good / 46.1% needs-improvement / 9.9% poor. The Apr 29–May 2 CLS sprint took mobile CLS p75 from 0.30 → 0.06, but targeted mobile-specific elements only (cart freegifts, Reamaze launcher position, Klaviyo popup positioning). Desktop never got the same treatment — its 0.20 is the worst-performing field metric on the site.

Likely desktop-specific contributors (NOT covered by the mobile sprint):
- Hover-state layout changes on mega-menu / category nav
- Wider image slots loading without reserved aspect-ratio
- Sidebar widgets (LoyaltyLion launcher, Reamaze, Klaviyo embedded forms) appearing late
- LimeSpot recommendation strips inserting after hero on PDP/homepage
- Vertex AI rec rails (if any wired)
- Header search popdown or dropdown opening without containment
- Sticky elements (sticky add-to-cart, sticky header) recomputing on scroll/resize

Tooling already in place:
- web-vitals-reporter.liquid pushes debug_target on every CLS event (page element selector of the shifted node)
- GA4 custom dimension debug_target is registered + populated
- scripts/web-vitals-report.py exists with --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop options
- Reference: bd memory 'ga4-cls-poor-events-7d-baseline-as-of' shows the mobile breakdown pattern that drove the May 2 sprint

Out of scope: mobile CLS work (already good at 0.06); INP/LCP investigation; the kt0 cart-drawer regression (separately fixed in commit ff1ef80).

Phase 1 — DATA (RO, no code changes):
1. Run `GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --days 7 --top 30 --device desktop` (add --device filter if not supported, extend the script).
2. Compare desktop top contributors vs mobile baseline (memory 'ga4-cls-poor-events-7d-baseline-as-of'). Surface the desktop-specific debug_targets that don't appear in mobile or have disproportionately higher desktop counts.
3. For each top-3 desktop contributor: identify the structural cause by inspecting the element in the live theme (Liquid file + CSS rules + JS that touches it). Categorize: late-load injection / unreserved image space / hover-state reflow / sidebar widget / sticky-element recalc / other.

Phase 2 — FIX (RW, one fix per dispatch — per Free-tier scope discipline, even though we're paid tier, this keeps the kt0-killer auditable):
For each top contributor, the standard fix patterns are:
- **Late-load widget injecting into flow** → reserve space with min-height or aspect-ratio at the parent (NOT contain: layout on a parent of fixed descendants — see kt0 lesson in .opencode_hints).
- **Image without dimensions** → add width/height attributes + aspect-ratio CSS.
- **Hover-state reflow** → use transform: translate (no layout) instead of margin/padding.
- **Sidebar widget appearing late** → reserve placeholder space sized to the widget's known dimensions.
- **Sticky-element recompute** → use position: sticky correctly (no JS-driven top/bottom changes).

Phase 3 — VERIFY:
1. Run `python3 scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py --config=scripts/smoke-test-drawers.config.json --theme=draft` — must still PASS (no cart drawer regression).

4. Acceptance verified at +28d when CrUX rolling window catches up.

Hard rules:
- Do NOT modify mobile-targeted fixes (the May 2 work is correct as-is).
- Do NOT add contain: layout to any parent of a fixed/absolute descendant (see kt0 lesson + .opencode_hints 'CSS containment + position gotchas').

Root cause: the CSS scoping (.homepage-blog .grid.grid--uniform { display: flex }) was correct, but the existing Pipeline 6 grid relies on width-class floats (.one-third / .medium-up--one-third etc.) on .grid__item children. When the parent flips to display:flex and items lose 'float: none', the width classes (which are floats with percentages) don't translate to flex-basis — items default to content-size, can't fit a row, wrap each to its own line → vertical stack with full-width images. 3. Stay with the JS approach but defer it more aggressively (IntersectionObserver already gates it; the layout cost is small if homepage-blog is below the fold).

The reverted commit's intent was correct (eliminate forced layout reads); the implementation under-specified the grid math. Re-dispatch should be scoped to a SINGLE template/page where the .homepage-blog class is used as intended, with manual visual verification on every page that includes the section before push.

title: Fix missing metric_rating param on 50-60% of CWV RUM events
description:
Dashboard's 'unknown' rating bucket shows ~50-60% of CWV events arriving without metric_rating populated. This is separate from the template param fix (vux, commit 64a0a6b) which is now working. The defensive 'unknown' bucket surfacing shipped with vux made the gap visible.

Symptoms:
- web-vitals-reporter.liquid pushes metric_rating to dataLayer for each CWV event (good/needs-improvement/poor based on web-vitals.js thresholds)
- Dashboard query reads metric_rating from GA4 event params
- GA4 shows 50-60% of CWV events with metric_rating = (not set)

Likely cause (analogous to template fix in vux): GTM CWV tag (or per-metric tags) may not be mapping a 'metric_rating' event parameter from dataLayer → GA4 event params. The template fix wired one DLV; metric_rating needs the same treatment but for a different field.

Investigation needed:
1. Inspect GTM live container — find the GA4 CWV event tag(s) (one per metric: LCP, CLS, INP, FCP, TTFB, or one combined). Check event parameters table for 'metric_rating' mapping.

title: Automated drawer smoke test (Layer 3 — Playwright-based kt0-killer gate)
description: Today's kt0 cart-drawer regression (commit ff1ef80) was a structural bug pattern that's destined to recur: a parent selector gets a CSS property that silently creates a containing block (contain:layout, transform, filter, etc.), and a position:fixed descendant gets trapped inside the parent's bounds instead of overlaying the viewport. Layer 1+2 (already shipped in commit d0334b0) added author-time guardrails and a manual pre-push smoke checklist to .opencode_hints. This bd ships Layer 3 — a deterministic automated test that catches kt0-class regressions BEFORE live push, converting the manual checklist into a CI-grade gate.
design:
## Design decisions LOCKED (do not re-litigate)

1. **Browser automation tool: Playwright (Python).**
   - Production-grade, headless, fast (~2-5s/test).
   - Other options ruled out: Chrome MCP (session-coupled, wrong for CI gate), cursor-agent (wrong tool category).
   - Setup: `pip install playwright && playwright install chromium`. Document in script's docstring + .opencode_hints.

2. **Auth: `?preview_theme_id=<id>` query param.**
   - No Shopify Storefront API token needed.
   - Default test target: draft theme `140785582179`.
   - Allow `--theme=live` for live-theme verification too (uses `131664707683`).

3. **Assertion set per overlay (the kt0-killer set).**
   For each overlay test:
   a. Trigger click opens the overlay within 2s timeout
   b. Panel's `position` computed style is `fixed` (or `absolute` with `<body>` as containing block)
   c. Panel `getBoundingClientRect()`: height >= 0.9 * viewport.height AND width >= 300px
   d. **Containment audit**: walk panel's parent chain to `<body>`. Test FAILS if ANY ancestor has:
      - `contain: layout | paint | strict | content`
      - `transform != 'none'`
      - `filter != 'none'`
      - `backdrop-filter != 'none'`
      - `perspective != 'none'`
      - `will-change` containing `transform` or `filter`
      This is the kt0-killer assertion — it catches the structural bug pattern.
   e. On fail: capture screenshot to `/tmp/smoke-fail-<test>-<ts>.png` and log the offending ancestor selector chain.

## Overlay tests to implement

| # | Test | Page | Trigger | Panel selector | State indicator |
|---|------|------|---------|----------------|-----------------|
| 1 | Cart drawer | `/products/davines-naturaltech-purifying-shampoo-for-scalp-with-oily-or-dry-dandruff` | `[data-drawer-toggle="drawer-cart"]` | `.cart__drawer .drawer__content` | `.drawer--visible` class on `.cart__drawer` |
| 2 | Mobile nav drawer | `/` (viewport: 375×667, mobile) | `[data-drawer-toggle="drawer-nav-mobile"]` or similar — INSPECT FIRST via Chrome MCP or grep sections/header.liquid for the trigger; document the exact selector found | `.nav__drawer .drawer__content` | `.drawer--visible` |
| 3 | PhotoSwipe lightbox | same PDP as #1 | `[data-zoom-button]` (first one) | `.pswp` (the photoswipe root) | `.pswp--open` class |
| 4 | Search overlay | `/` | `[data-search-toggle]` or `.header__search-toggle` — INSPECT FIRST and document | `.search__form-wrapper` or `.search__overlay` | aria-expanded / visible class — INSPECT FIRST |

For tests #2 and #4, the senior-developer must INSPECT the live theme HTML (via `curl` + grep, or via the rendered HTML in `/tmp/cart.html` from earlier session work) to identify the exact trigger + panel + state-class selectors. Do NOT hardcode selectors that may not exist. If a selector cannot be confirmed for a given test, log a skip + warning rather than failing — the goal is high signal, not false positives.

## Script architecture

**File:** `scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py` (~200 LoC including docstring)

**CLI:**

## Implementation notes for the senior-developer

- Use `playwright.sync_api.sync_playwright()` for simplicity (sync API is easier to read than async for this use case).
- Run with `headless=True` by default; `--headless=false` for visual debugging.
- Use `page.wait_for_selector(state_indicator, timeout=2000)` for the open-state check.
- For the containment audit, evaluate JavaScript in the page context:
  ```python
  result = page.evaluate("""
      (panelSelector) => {
          const panel = document.querySelector(panelSelector);
          if (!panel) return {ok: false, reason: 'panel not found'};
          let el = panel.parentElement;
          while (el && el !== document.body) {
              const cs = getComputedStyle(el);
              if (cs.contain && /\\b(layout|paint|strict|content)\\b/.test(cs.contain)) return {ok: false, reason: `contain: ${cs.contain}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.transform && cs.transform !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `transform: ${cs.transform}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.filter && cs.filter !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `filter: ${cs.filter}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.backdropFilter && cs.backdropFilter !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `backdrop-filter: ${cs.backdropFilter}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.perspective && cs.perspective !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `perspective: ${cs.perspective}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.willChange && /transform|filter/.test(cs.willChange)) return {ok: false, reason: `will-change: ${cs.willChange}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              el = el.parentElement;
          }
          function getSelectorPath(node) {
              if (!node) return '';
              const parts = [];
              while (node && node !== document.body) {
                  let s = node.tagName.toLowerCase();
                  if (node.id) s += '#' + node.id;
                  if (node.className && typeof node.className === 'string') s += '.' + node.className.trim().replace(/\\s+/g, '.');
                  parts.unshift(s);
                  node = node.parentElement;
              }
              return parts.join(' > ');
          }
          return {ok: true};
      }
  """, panel_selector)
  ```
- Allow the script to be run in dev (Python 3.10+, no other deps).

title: Codify CSS containment gotchas + pre-push smoke checklist (prevent kt0-class regressions)
description:
Today's kt0 CLS hotfix (ff1ef80) was caused by an author-time misunderstanding of how `contain: layout` interacts with fixed/absolute descendants. The CSS rule's own comment incorrectly claimed "contain:layout is no-visual-impact" — but the property actually establishes a containing block for fixed/absolute descendants per CSS Containment spec. That trapped the cart drawer's `position: fixed; z-index: 20050` inside `.header__wrapper`, causing a 24+ hour live regression where the drawer rendered inline instead of overlaying the viewport.

Bug surfaced via user report; would have been caught instantly by either:
  (a) An author-time guardrail listing the CSS properties that create containing blocks for fixed/absolute descendants, OR
  (b) A merge-gate smoke checklist requiring drawer-open verification on draft before live push.

This bd ships BOTH guardrails as documentation-only updates to .opencode_hints (the project-root file already read by all offload-dispatched agents), plus a `bd remember` entry so the lesson is searchable via `bd memories contain`.

### Addition 1 — new section "CSS containment + position gotchas"

Insert before the final "## Project context" section. Verbatim content to add:

```
## CSS containment + position gotchas

Adding any of the following CSS properties to a selector silently creates a
new containing block for `position: fixed` and `position: absolute`
descendants. If you apply one of these to a parent that contains a fixed
overlay (cart drawer, mobile nav, search modal, Klaviyo popup, LoyaltyLion
notification, PWA install banner, cookie consent), the descendant's
positioning resolves relative to that parent — NOT the viewport — and the
overlay renders inline within the parent's bounds instead of covering the
viewport.

This is the bug pattern that broke the cart drawer on 2026-05-11 (the kt0
fix's `contain: layout` on `.header__wrapper` trapped the cart-drawer's
fixed position inside the header wrapper's bounds; see commit ff1ef80 and
`bd memories contain` for the post-mortem).

| Property                          | Effect on fixed/absolute descendants                        |
|-----------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
| `contain: layout`                 | Containing block + new stacking context                     |
| `contain: paint`                  | Same as layout + clips overflow                             |
| `contain: strict` / `content`     | Includes both layout and paint                              |
| `transform: <anything>` (not `none`) | Containing block + new stacking context                  |
| `filter: <anything>`              | Same                                                        |
| `backdrop-filter: <anything>`     | Same                                                        |
| `perspective: <anything>`         | Same                                                        |
| `will-change: transform`          | Same (pre-emptive)                                          |
| `will-change: filter`             | Same                                                        |
| `overflow: hidden\|auto\|scroll`  | Doesn't create containing block, but clips fixed children   |
|                                   | visually if they exceed bounds (similar symptom)            |

**Required audit before adding any of these to a parent selector:**

```
# What position:fixed / position:absolute is inside the targeted selector?
grep -rn "position:\s*fixed\|position:\s*absolute" sections/ snippets/ \
  assets/theme.css assets/theme.dev.js | grep -i "<selector-scope>"
```

If the audit surfaces any fixed/absolute descendants, either:
- Move the descendant OUT of the selector's scope (DOM restructure), OR
- Narrow the selector to a sibling element that does NOT contain the descendant, OR
- Skip the containment fix (accept the CLS cost)

DO NOT trust comments in existing CSS that claim `contain: layout` is
"no visual impact" or "purely a hint". That phrasing was the source of
the kt0 regression — it's actively wrong.
```

description:
GSC mobile CWV (5/10) flags 4 URL groups with LCP > 2.5s; the same 4 URL groups also appear in CLS-failing list:

| URL group | URLs | LCP | CLS |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| /collections/products-for-rebonded-hair | 155 | 2.6s | 0.16 |
| /blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas | 152 | 2.8s | 0.22 |
| /blogs/hair-education/purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines | 136 | 2.9s | (not flagged) |
| /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu | 20 | 3.0s | 0.21 |
| /collections/kerastase-heat-protectors | 20 | (not flagged) | 0.12 |

The blog posts (152 + 136 URLs) are already covered by article-content-with-img-dims.liquid + the 7tq LCP-priority preload. If they're STILL at 2.8-2.9s, the residual gap is either the hero image itself or a specific markup pattern that doesn't match the snippet's parser.

The /pages/ page (azta-urban-salon-service-menu) is at /pages/, NOT /blogs/ — its `page.content` doesn't go through article-content-with-img-dims (which is rendered only from sections/article.liquid). Same dimension-injection treatment likely needed for sections/page.liquid.

Collection pages (products-for-rebonded-hair, kerastase-heat-protectors) need separate investigation — their LCP element is typically the first product card image; possible causes: hero banner, deep grid, slow filter logic.
design:
## Phase A — extend dimension injection to /pages/ (Shopify pages)

The existing snippet at snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid is parameterised on `content` — re-use it for page.content too.

1. Inspect sections/page.liquid (or wherever page.content is rendered).
2. Replace `{{ page.content }}` with `{% render 'article-content-with-img-dims', content: page.content %}`.
3. Repeat for any other page-template variants.

Validation: visit /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu, view-source, confirm body images now have width/height attrs from URL pattern.

For best-hair-color-for-morenas + purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines:
1. Confirm article-content-with-img-dims is firing (view-source check).
2. PSI trace — what's the LCP element? If it's the hero, check the article-hero-with-fetchpriority pattern from issue 9hw.
3. If hero is fine, look for something blocking render: heavy script, large CSS stylesheet, slow font.

Files (Phase A scope only):
- sections/page.liquid (add render call)
- possibly templates/page.*.json if there are multiple page templates

Phase B + C are separate read-only investigation tasks (can be batched into one bd later).

title: BOGOS app: 2,721 'reduce of empty array' errors / 7d from apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com/js/app.js:277:3
description: JS error pipeline (post-l3g fix, 2026-05-06) surfaced this as the #4 most frequent error type by count. Real bug in BOGOS's code — they call .reduce() on what can be an empty array. Two-track: vendor support email + theme-side gate so BOGOS doesn't init on empty cart.
design:
## Goal
Address ~2,721 "reduce of empty array" errors / 7d coming from BOGOS app's bundle (apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com/js/app.js:277:3). Real production bug in their code triggered when cart has no items eligible for the offer (or cart is empty). Two tracks: vendor outreach + theme-side defer.

## What we know
- Error: `reduce of empty array with no initial value`
- Source: `https://apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com/js/app.js:277:3` (BOGOS CDN)
- Volume: 2,721 events / 7 days = ~390/day
- BOGOS-related issue 3uw (CLS) was already shipped 2026-05-04 — separate concern.

## Two-track action

### Track A — Vendor (admin)
1. Email BOGOS support (apps@makebecool.com or via Shopify app contact) with:
   - Error message + stack frame (app.js:277:3)
   - Reproduction: empty cart visiting any page that mounts BOGOS widget
   - Volume metric (~390/day, our shop)
   - Request: fix the .reduce() call to provide an initial value (`.reduce((acc, x) => ..., [])`).

### Track B — Theme-side defer (mechanical, OC-dispatchable)
Find the BOGOS include / SDK script tag in the theme. Add a guard so BOGOS doesn't initialize until cart has at least 1 item:
- Find: `grep -rn -iE "bogos|sca-gift|secomapp|conversion-bear" --include='*.liquid' .`
- Wrap the include/script in `{% if cart.item_count > 0 %}` (or equivalent JS-side guard).
- This avoids the empty-array reduce error AND saves bundle weight on empty-cart pages.

Risk: if BOGOS provides a banner / call-to-action on empty cart, gating breaks that. Audit before shipping.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/bogos-* (or wherever the app embed lives)
- layout/theme.liquid (if include lives there)

## Out of scope
- Replacing BOGOS — there's a separate parked issue ur0 for custom BOGOS replacement (1 day, $420/yr saved). This is a defensive fix only.
acceptance_criteria:
After fix:
- BOGOS "reduce of empty array" error count drops from 2,721/7d → 0 (or near-zero)
- Tracked via dashboard JS errors section
- No regression on BOGOS widget functionality on cart with items
- Vendor support ticket filed (CC the response/ticket# in bd notes)

Dispatched to OC lead-architect via /offload — new SOP, free-tier opencode/minimax-m2.5-free. Track B (theme-side empty-cart guard) only — Track A vendor email is admin/user track. Brief explicitly forbids git commit/push (CC merge-gate).

title: Legacy tab content unescape — render escaped HTML in product-tabs without merchant re-save
description: After 0ru shipped, tab content saved pre-fix renders escaped (legacy richtext-stored data). Adding a defensive heuristic-gated unescape pass to product-tabs.liquid avoids requiring the merchant to re-save every product manually.
design:
## Goal
Defensive Liquid filter to render legacy tab content that was HTML-escaped by the old `richtext` schema before issue 0ru shipped (commit c6cc2a0, 2026-05-06). Without this, every product whose tab content was authored before the fix shows literal `<ol>` / `<strong>` etc. as text — merchant must re-save each product manually.

## Context (read first, do not skip)
- Issue 0ru changed schema for raw_content_1..5 from "richtext" → "html" in sections/product.liquid + section-product.liquid.
- New edits work fine. Old data — Shopify stored it pre-escaped (`<ol><li>...`) when the schema was richtext.
- After the schema change, that escaped string passes through `{{ _content }}` and renders the literal characters (visible markup).
- Issue 0ru's resolution noted this as a follow-up: "Bug 3 caveat: existing escaped tab content stays escaped after schema change — merchant must re-save each affected product. If many products affected, follow-up with a bulk-unescape ticket."

## Target file
- snippets/product-tabs.liquid — single file. Two render paths inside: `tabs` style block (~line 51) and `accordions` style block (~line 102).

## What to do
Add a defensive unescape branch to the existing `_resolved_content` chain that the metafield-syntax detector lives in (already added by 0ru). The unescape should ONLY trigger when the content "looks escaped" — heuristic detection to avoid double-decoding legitimate entities.

Heuristic detection (conservative):
- Content contains `<` AND `>` AND at least one of `<ol`, `<ul`, `<li`, `<p`, `<div`, `<strong`, `<em`, `<br` (lowercased substring match).
- Content does NOT contain `<` followed by an alpha character (i.e. no real HTML tags already present — that would suggest mixed/double-encoded content, which we should NOT touch).

If heuristic matches, run a bounded unescape chain:
- `replace: '<', '<'`
- `replace: '>', '>'`
- `replace: '"', '"'`
- `replace: ''', "'"`
- `replace: '&', '&'` (LAST — must come after others so we don't double-decode)

Apply after the existing metafield-syntax detection logic — the metafield resolver returns rendered HTML which doesn't need unescape, so the unescape pass should run on `_resolved_content` only IF no metafield resolution happened.

Apply in BOTH `tabs` AND `accordions` paths (lines ~51-66 and ~102-119 — same structure, both need it).

## Invariants to preserve
- DO NOT modify the existing metafield-syntax resolver from 0ru.
- DO NOT touch sections/product.liquid or section-product.liquid (schema is correct now).
- DO NOT add unescape logic elsewhere in the codebase — bounded to product-tabs.liquid.
- The heuristic must NOT match content that already contains real HTML — that's a sign of double-encoding which we should NOT decode.

## How to verify
1. python3 -m py_compile is N/A (Liquid file).
2. Push to draft theme 140785582179 with `--only=snippets/product-tabs.liquid` (the coordinator will do this — you do NOT push).
3. Coordinator will smoke test on:
   - A product that has `<ol><li>` content authored pre-fix (will need to find one — likely the No.4 Fine Bond Maintenance Shampoo from the original screenshot).
   - A product that has metafield syntax (`{{ product.metafields...}}`) — must still resolve correctly.
   - A new product with native HTML — must render correctly with no double-decode.

## Failure modes to avoid
- Double-decoding: if a product had a literal `&` in its content for a real ampersand, our chain would convert that to `&` then still match the heuristic on the next pass... wait, the heuristic runs ONCE per render so this is fine.
- Over-eager match: if a product's content discusses HTML in plain text (e.g. "use the <strong> tag for bold"), the heuristic would unescape it. That's an acceptable false positive — the merchant can put real `<strong>` tags instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- Existing pre-0ru products with escaped tab content render as proper HTML (lists, bold, paragraphs, NOT literal `<ol>`).
- Products with metafield syntax (`{{ product.metafields.hmnl.* }}`) continue to resolve to actual metafield values.
- Products with native HTML (post-0ru, properly authored) render unchanged.
- No double-decoding regression — products with intentional HTML-encoded entities in body text render correctly.
- Diff scope: ONLY snippets/product-tabs.liquid modified. No JSON schema changes, no section file changes.
notes:
Dispatched to OC lead-architect via /offload — new SOP (sub-orchestrator pattern), free-tier opencode/minimax-m2.5-free

title: VisualQuizBuilder app embed gating — 447KB on every mobile pageload
description: VQB (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com, ~447KB JS) loads on every mobile pageload despite being used only on quiz routes. ASSUMPTION (per user 2026-05-12): VQB admin offers no per-template gating — manual theme-side intervention required. Investigate JS interception pattern to block VQB scripts on non-quiz routes while preserving full functionality on /apps/quiz.
design:
**Goal:** Block VisualQuizBuilder (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com, ~447KB JS) from loading on non-quiz pages, given that VQB admin offers no per-template gating and we cannot edit the app embed itself.

**Approach (theme-side JS interception):** Inject a small guard script EARLY in `layout/theme.liquid` (before the app-embed slot renders) that:
- On non-quiz routes (`pathname` does NOT match `/apps/quiz` or `/pages/quiz*` or any other VQB-bearing route — to be discovered), prevents script tags pointing at `cdn.visualquizbuilder.com` (and any other VQB origin) from executing.
- Most robust patterns: (a) `MutationObserver` watching `head` + `body` for inserted `<script>` nodes and removing them before they fetch, OR (b) override `HTMLScriptElement.prototype.src` setter / `document.createElement` to filter. Pattern (a) is preferred because it doesn't break global JS contracts.
- Preserves full VQB functionality on quiz routes — must NOT touch the embed there.

**Investigation tasks for OC (read-only, no code changes):**

1. **Discover VQB injection mechanism.** Open a non-quiz page (e.g. homepage), capture network waterfall + DOM scripts. Identify:
   - Exact script src URL(s) and all origins involved (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com plus any auxiliary domains).
   - WHERE the script tag appears in the DOM (head, body, position relative to theme.liquid markers).
   - WHEN it's injected (sync in app-embed slot? async via a loader? lazy via IntersectionObserver?).
   - Whether VQB sets any `window.*` properties BEFORE the main bundle loads (would constrain interception ordering).
2. **Discover the quiz route(s).** Walk the storefront for any URL that legitimately uses VQB (likely `/apps/quiz`, possibly `/pages/quiz`, plus PDP-embedded quiz widgets if any). The allowlist must be precise — false-negative breaks the product.
3. **Confirm app-embed slot location.** Find where in `layout/theme.liquid` the `{% render_app_blocks %}` or equivalent slot renders, so the guard script can be placed strictly BEFORE it.
4. **Propose intervention.** Write a ~30-line guard snippet (do NOT commit it, paste into bd notes). Include the pathname allowlist regex, the MutationObserver filter, and the origin denylist. Justify the ordering choice.
5. **Risk audit.** Enumerate failure modes: VQB script bundles other vendors (would also be killed), VQB loaded via Web Component / iframe (MutationObserver might miss it), VQB injected post-load via async fetcher (guard must persist). For each, propose a mitigation or a check.

**Out of scope for this investigation:**
- Any code commit. This is research only.
- Modifying VQB admin settings (assumed unavailable per user direction).
- Replacing VQB with a different quiz vendor (separate evaluation).

**Deliverable:** bd notes appended with sections (1)–(5) above. Include a `git status`/`git diff --stat` confirming zero changes were staged.

title: Investigate + fix desktop CLS p75 still at 0.20 (POOR; mobile dropped to 0.07)

design:
## Goal
Find why desktop CrUX CLS p75 = 0.20 (POOR threshold ≥0.10) didn't improve like mobile. The Apr 29–May 2 CLS sprint shipped 8 fixes that took mobile field CLS p75 from 0.30 → 0.07. Desktop didn't budge.

## Hypothesis
The CLS sprint's targets were mobile-specific:
- Cart freegifts 160 px reserve (mobile cart layout)
- Reamaze widget label position:fixed (mobile widget)
- Klaviyo + LoyaltyLion overlays position:fixed (mobile overlays)
- Shop Sheriff PWA modal hide (mobile install prompt)
- Header announcement min-height (likely mobile-specific)

Desktop probably has its OWN unique CLS sources we never measured because mobile dominated the field-data attention.

## Investigation steps
1. Open homepage in Brave with desktop viewport (1920x1080).
2. DevTools → Performance Insights → record load + idle phase.
3. Inspect Layout Shift events — note source elements + shift score.
4. Repeat for /products/* (PDP), /collections/* (collection grid), /cart, /blogs/* (article).
5. Cross-reference with GA4 desktop RUM (filter device_category=desktop).
6. Look for desktop-specific late-loading widgets: BSS B2B portal scripts (might still hydrate on desktop), LimeSpot widgets, Smart SEO injections.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid (aspect-ratio reserves)
- assets/theme.dev.js / theme.js (defer / lazy-init for desktop-only widgets)
- specific section files (one per identified shift source)

## NOT dispatchable
DevTools-based investigation. Keep on CC + browser MCP.
acceptance_criteria:
- CrUX desktop CLS p75 drops from 0.20 → ≤0.10 (28-day window post-fix)
- No mobile CLS regression (currently 0.07; must stay ≤0.10)
- Specific shift sources documented in commit message + bd notes

title: Tree-shake vendor.js (Rellax/axios/ellipsis baggage) — needs build-pipeline access
description:
vendor.js is 100KB on disk (~30-35KB gzipped), bundles Rellax (~10KB, only used when conditional [data-rellax] present — never on homepage), axios (~8KB, can be replaced with native fetch — most call sites already use fetch), and ellipsis (~2KB, used by truncate widget on PDP/article only). Total ~20KB JS savings. theme.js line 7243 IIFE pulls these vendor bundles in even when unused; the cost is parse + initial heap allocation, not runtime calls.

Why blocked: this repo only has the BUILT vendor.js — the source is in a separate build pipeline (esbuild config + node_modules) we don't have access to. Tree-shake requires editing the source, rebuilding, and pushing the regenerated vendor.js.

When unblocked: estimated -50 to -100ms parse-time on cold cache, -1ms or so on warm cache. Worth doing once build access is available, but not the highest-leverage perf win without it.

title: /cart INP 21% poor — investigate quantity stepper / drawer interactions
description: 2026-05-02 dashboard: /cart shows 12/58 (21%) poor-INP events in 7d, second-highest %poor INP page after PDPs. Cart UX is interaction-heavy (qty steppers, drawer toggles, line-item remove). Likely synchronous JS work on click handlers — quantity update fetches, then re-renders cart, then re-fires totals/freegifts/sticky-bar logic. Investigation: GA4 INP attribution targets filtered to pagePath=/cart, find which target dominates. Then audit the click handler chain. Fixes typically: yield to main thread between updates, debounce qty steppers, defer non-critical re-render (sticky bar, freegifts) to requestIdleCallback.
notes:
2026-05-04 STATIC ANALYSIS (no DevTools): Investigation complete.

ATTRIBUTION (last 14d, /cart, poor INP only):
  17 / 22 poor INPs → '#shopify-section-template--XXX__main>div.cart__template>div.wrapper.pt2.pb4'
  This is the cart body container — generic ancestor of all qty steppers, remove buttons,
  drawer toggles. The browser couldn't isolate a more specific target, which means the slow
  handler is either (a) attached at this container level via delegation, or (b) the click
  bubbles to a slow document/body listener registered by a third-party app.

CLICK → PAINT CHAIN (sync work in INP window):
  Source: assets/theme.dev.js (readable copy of bundled assets/theme.js).
  1. Ns.increase/decrease (line 4633): preventDefault + value++ + input.dispatchEvent('change') SYNC
  2. CartItems.initInputs change listener (line 4083): parseInt + closest + lockState + updateCart
  3. lockState (line 4111):
       - this.latestClick.querySelector('.item--loadbar').style.display = 'block'   (1 style write)
       - this.loader.classList.add('cart--loading')                                  (1 class add)
  4. updateCart returns immediately (fetch is async — NOT in INP window)
  5. Browser must paint → forced style recalc

INP COSTS (in priority order of suspicion):
  A. Broad CSS cascade from .cart--loading. Three rules (.cart--loading .quantity__wrapper,
     .quantity__input, .quantity__button) match ALL N items × 3 = ~3N elements. Adding the
     class on the high-level loader wrapper forces O(N) style recalc.
  B. External app listeners on input/change events: BSS B2B price recalc, BOGOS gift state,
     LoyaltyLion redeem — all run synchronously in the same tick.
  C. Document-level delegated click handlers from other apps (less likely).

RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP (live DevTools):
  1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/cart in Chrome with full cart (~5-10 items).
  2. Open DevTools → Performance → Record.
  3. Click +/- on a quantity stepper.
  4. Stop recording. Look for the Long Task on the click.
  5. Expand the click → Recalculate Style segment. Note the duration + 'elements affected' count.
  6. Inspect the Event listeners panel for [data-quantity-input] inputs — see all attached
     listeners (theme + BSS B2B + BOGOS + LoyaltyLion). The non-theme listeners are the targets.

LOW-RISK FIX CANDIDATES (in order of safety):
  1. CSS-only: scope .cart--loading rules to the active row only. Not trivial without JS change.
  2. JS bundle patch (theme.js + theme.dev.js): in CartItems.lockState, add 'cart--loading'
     to this.latestClick instead of this.loader. CSS .cart--loading .quantity__* rules then
     only match within ONE row → O(1) recalc instead of O(N). Low semantic risk, but minified
     bundle modification → must test on draft theme 140785582179 first.
  3. Yield-to-paint shim in custom-theme.js: intercept stepper +/- clicks in capturing phase,
     wrap dispatchEvent('change') in requestAnimationFrame so click handler returns before
     downstream change listeners run. Lets the browser paint first. Risk: races with theme.js
     handler attachment timing.

NOT RECOMMENDED THIS SESSION:
  Any modification of theme.js without DevTools-validated profile. Cart is a high-stakes
  page (conversion-critical) and silent regressions on stepper/remove behavior are costly.
  Static analysis points strongly at fix #2 above, but ship only after DevTools confirms
  Recalculate Style is the dominant cost.

title: Lazy-init below-fold Swipers on homepage via IntersectionObserver
description:
Production INP attribution (RUM 2026-04-29) shows homepage at 67% good / 13.1% poor — worst on site. 97% of poor INP events have (not set) debug_target = page-load JS work blocking first interaction.

Homepage has 8 Swiper instances initializing at DOMContentLoaded:
  - 7 .swiper.collection (custom-theme.js:14292)
  - 1 banner-slider (separate init)
  - 315 .swiper-slide elements total

Each Swiper does layout measurement + touch/pointer/keyboard listener binding at init. Doing this for 8 swipers serially in one task is the most likely source of the (not set) INPs.

PROPOSED FIX:
Add a tiny IntersectionObserver wrapper that defers new Swiper(...) for below-fold .swiper.collection elements until they scroll into the viewport. The first slider (above fold) keeps eager init for visual polish; the other 6-7 init lazily.

Implementation options:
A. Modify the custom-theme.js bundle itself (requires source repo + build pipeline — likely not in this repo)
B. Add a wrapper snippet that runs BEFORE custom-theme.js on DOMContentLoaded, removing the .swiper.collection class from below-fold elements, then restoring + triggering the init manually when they intersect. Brittle but doable from theme code only.
C. Add a snippet that disables Swiper auto-init by removing .collection from class, then re-adds + manually calls Swiper constructor on intersect. Needs Swiper to be exposed globally — currently it's IIFE-bundled inside custom-theme.js.

RECOMMENDED: Option B with extensive testing. Estimated win: 200-400ms TBT reduction on homepage initial load = direct INP improvement (since INP is bounded by main-thread availability).

VALIDATION: Re-run web-vitals-report.py --metric INP --by page after 14d to confirm homepage INP %good moves from 67% toward 75% target.
close_reason: IntersectionObserver wrapper live on both draft and live theme. 6 of 7 .swiper.collection instances now lazy-init 200px before viewport entry.
