title: Elevar /a/elevar tracking endpoint takes 2.6s on every PSI run
description:
2026-04-28 PSI diagnostic showed /a/elevar (Elevar's GTM-proxy / event-collection endpoint at the shop's own domain) consistently taking 2,634ms — the single biggest network-time hog on the homepage.

Elevar proxies tracking events through the shop's own domain (under /a/elevar) to bypass ad-blockers and 3rd-party-cookie restrictions. Slow response from this endpoint adds to TBT (because subsequent JS waits on the response) and overall page load time.

Diagnostic data (/tmp/psi-result.json from 2026-04-28 morning):
  - URL pattern: /a/elevar?source_url=...
  - Method: Fetch
  - Time: 2,634ms
  - Transfer size: 1KB

Next steps:
1. Open Elevar dashboard → check event-collection latency metrics. Compare HairMNL vs Elevar's average client.
2. Email Elevar support: 'Why is /a/elevar consistently 2.6s on PSI? Can we narrow this?' Include the diagnostic data.
3. Consider whether the Elevar proxy is necessary, or if direct GTM (without Elevar's proxy) would be faster. Trade-off: ad-blockers will block direct GTM more, losing tracking signal.
4. If Elevar can't speed up: defer the Elevar script load further, or fire events on requestIdleCallback only (not on page load).

Admin / vendor-side work, not theme code. ~30 min initial investigation.
notes: 2026-05-01 investigation: Elevar loads via Shopify app embed (content_for_header) as <script type='module' dynamic>. Three chained awaits: config.js → handler JS → /a/elevar tracking endpoint (the 2.6s call). Since type=module is inherently deferred, this does NOT block HTML parsing or rendering — the 2.6s is Elevar's server response time, not a theme-side blocking issue. No theme code change possible. Action items: (1) Check Elevar admin for deferred/idle firing mode; (2) Email Elevar support with the diagnostic data — 2,634ms is far above acceptable latency; (3) If Elevar can't fix: consider firing GTM's Elevar tags on requestIdleCallback via GTM trigger config. Reassigning to admin/vendor track.

title: Cloudflare bot challenge fires on rapid add-to-cart sequences (admin-side, not theme)
description:
Reported 2026-04-28 night: shoppers adding several items rapidly trigger Cloudflare's bot/rate-limit verification screen. Once challenged, /cart/add.js POSTs may be blocked or delayed, and theme.js asset loading may also be affected, leaving the cart drawer in a broken state.

This is NOT a theme code issue — Cloudflare is rate-limiting based on request volume per IP per window. The theme can mitigate the post-challenge UX (see hairmnl-theme-dc2 follow-up: drawer-toggle intercept rewritten to be self-contained, no longer depends on theme.js binding handlers), but cannot prevent the challenge itself.

Admin-side fixes to investigate (Shopify admin → Online Store → Domains → Cloudflare config, or via Cloudflare dashboard if account-managed):
1. Whitelist /cart/add.js, /cart/change.js, /cart.js endpoints from bot detection — these are AJAX endpoints that legitimate users hit several times per session.
2. Reduce 'Bot Fight Mode' or 'Under Attack Mode' sensitivity for those paths.
3. Set up a Page Rule that bypasses the WAF for these paths.
4. Consider increasing the rate-limit threshold or adjusting the window.

Theme-side mitigations already shipped today:
- Self-contained drawer-toggle intercept (so cart click still works even if theme.js loading is throttled)
- Liquid-side conditional render of cart message (no JS dependency for correct initial render)

Theme-side mitigations NOT shipped (would require build):
- Client-side throttle on add-to-cart button (e.g., disable for 500 ms after click). Risk: shoppers click multiple times thinking nothing happened, worsens UX.
- Optimistic UI updates that don't depend on /cart.js after each add. Possible but invasive.

Worth a 30-min admin session to check Cloudflare config.

title: Cart drawer dual-message bug: success + default both render when subtotal >= free-shipping threshold
description:
When cart subtotal qualifies for free shipping, the drawer's promo banner shows BOTH .cart__message-success ('Congratulations! Your order qualifies for free shipping') AND .cart__message-default ('Spend ₱-1,150 more...') simultaneously, concatenated as one long line. Expected: only .cart__message-success visible per cart.css line 43-45 rule: .cart__message.is-success .cart__message-success ~ .cart__message-default { display: none } Observed (2026-04-28 night, hotfix-confirmed-shipped state): both visible. Likely cause: the AJAX drawer-rerender path at theme.js:5205 (renderPopdown) replaces drawer.innerHTML with freshly-rendered Liquid fetched via ?section_id=api-product-popdown. The fresh fragment may render with class_message='' (cart state mismatch with Liquid render context) OR the freeShippingMessageHandle JS listener (theme.js:1737) doesn't re-fire after innerHTML swap. Diagnostic next steps: 1. Use Chrome MCP to inspect live DOM right after drawer opens — confirm whether parent

has is-success class 2. If is-success missing: check Liquid render context for the popdown section (cart state availability) 3. If is-success present: check for higher-specificity override or inline style 4. Consider robust fix: change cart-drawer.liquid lines 105-111 to conditionally render ONLY ONE span based on class_message, eliminating the CSS-toggle dependency. Note: this loses dynamic JS update; may need re-trigger of CartShippingMessage class on AJAX rerender.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Resolve duplicate sale labels on product cards (product__badge--custom vs product-labels system)
description: Pre-existing tension found during Judge.me draft smoke test 2026-04-27. Some product cards (e.g., AVEDA Nutriplenish on /collections/aveda or homepage) render TWO sale labels when compare_at_price > price: (1) the original .product__badge.product__badge--custom red ribbon with text like '10% Off' (custom per-product badge from theme system, possibly metafield-driven), and (2) the new .product-label.product-label--sale auto-computed '-10%' from snippets/product-labels.liquid (added in commit 06fd1d8 to replace BSS Product Labels). Decide: (a) remove the auto-computed sale label from product-labels.liquid (keeping it only for tag-based labels and Sold Out), letting the existing custom badge handle sale display; (b) remove the custom badge system entirely, migrate any per-product custom text to label:* tags, let product-labels handle everything; (c) keep both but visually de-duplicate via CSS so only one shows when both fire. Currently visible on draft theme 140785582179 only because draft was just synced; live theme renders only the custom badge because live's product-grid-item-branded.liquid is older and doesn't include the {% render 'product-labels' %} call.

(Won't-fix path) Keep current state. Founders/marketing keep free-form per-product badge text (e.g. '10% Off', 'free gift', 'sale', 'limited'). Lose auto-compute -X%. BSS Product Labels app already removed; perf win retained either way.

title: Periodic sync of admin-managed Shopify files (templates/*.json, config/settings_*.json) from live to git
description:
Shopify admin auto-edits these files every time someone changes settings in the Theme Editor (banner images, homepage section blocks, settings_schema additions, etc.):

- templates/*.json (notably templates/index.json — homepage banners + featured collections)
- config/settings_data.json
- config/settings_schema.json

Per .opencode_hints §4, agents must NOT edit these files (auto-clobbered by Shopify). But that policy leaves the GIT copies progressively stale as admin makes Theme Editor changes. The divergence surfaced today 2026-05-12:

- templates/index.json in git (from 2026-05-03 snapshot) referenced March-April 2026 banner image filenames; live had been rolled forward to May-June 2026 banners. When draft theme drift required re-pushing templates/index.json, the stale local copy would have re-introduced broken image refs. We worked around by pulling from live and pushing directly via --path, leaving git unchanged. That's a band-aid — git is now MORE stale.

- config/settings_schema.json was 7 bytes behind live (admin made a small settings change since 2026-05-02). Same band-aid applied.

Long-term solution: a scripted periodic pull from live into a separate 'admin-sync' branch (or a dedicated git operation) that snapshots these files weekly. Doesn't violate the no-edit rule because the source is live itself. Reduces emergency surface area when draft drift is detected.

Out of scope: agents editing these files via Theme Editor admin (still forbidden); merging admin changes back to live (admin is already the source-of-truth for these files via Shopify).
design:
Implementation spec for Cursor dispatch:

### Files to create/modify
- .github/workflows/admin-file-sync.yml (NEW file)
- scripts/admin-file-sync.sh (NEW file, called by the GHA workflow)

### What to build
A GitHub Actions workflow that runs weekly (Sunday 02:00 UTC) and on workflow_dispatch.

The workflow:
1. Checks out the repo
2. Installs Shopify CLI (shopify/shopify-cli-action or direct curl install — check what version is available)
3. Authenticates via SHOPIFY_CLI_PARTNERS_TOKEN secret (already set in repo secrets)
4. Pulls these files from live theme 131664707683 into a temp path:
   - templates/index.json
   - templates/product.json
   - templates/collection.json
   - templates/cart.json
   - config/settings_data.json
   - config/settings_schema.json
5. Compares each file against repo HEAD (git diff)
6. If any file differs, opens a PR or commits to a branch 'admin-sync/<date>' with a commit message like:
   chore: sync admin-managed theme files from live (YYYY-MM-DD)
7. The PR body lists which files changed and notes they are admin-managed (not to be hand-edited)

### Hard constraints
- Workflow MUST NOT auto-merge to main — create a PR or draft branch only
- Workflow MUST NOT push to live theme
- Use --nodelete flag on shopify theme pull so it doesn't delete local-only files
- SHOPIFY_STORE must be hairmnl.myshopify.com
- All secrets (SHOPIFY_CLI_PARTNERS_TOKEN, SHOPIFY_FLAG_STORE) assumed already present in repo secrets

### Acceptance criteria
1. .github/workflows/admin-file-sync.yml passes yamllint
2. Workflow file shows correct cron schedule, correct theme ID, correct file list
3. Script is executable (chmod +x scripts/admin-file-sync.sh) if separate script file used
4. git diff shows ONLY the 2 new files; no other files modified
acceptance_criteria:
1. .github/workflows/admin-sync.yml exists and runs weekly (chosen cadence, e.g., Sunday 02:00 UTC).
2. Workflow pulls listed admin files from live theme via Shopify CLI + commits to main with message 'chore(admin-sync): refresh admin-managed files from live'.
3. First successful run lands within 1 week of merge.
4. Documentation: ARCHITECTURE.md §3 or §4 updated to note the cron job exists and which files it owns.
5. Verification: after one successful run, ls -la with mtime shows the listed files were refreshed and git log shows the chore commit.

title: Complete smoke-test-drawers.py PhotoSwipe + search overlay coverage (currently SKIP)
description:
The Layer 3 smoke test (scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py, shipped in commit 6335c01) currently has 2 working tests (cart drawer, mobile nav) and 2 SKIPs marked with `incomplete_reason`:

1. PhotoSwipe lightbox on PDP — the zoom button (.media__zoom__icon) reveals only on parent .product__media hover. Programmatic click via headless Playwright doesn't reliably fire the PhotoSwipe init chain. Need a different trigger strategy (e.g., hover parent first, OR call the init JS directly via page.evaluate).

2. Search overlay on Home — #search-popdown opens as a 107px-tall top-anchored bar (intentional design — popdown, not full-viewport overlay). The default kt0-killer geometry assertion (height >= 90% viewport) is the wrong assertion shape for popdowns.

Manual smoke test (Layer 2 checklist items #3 and #4 in .opencode_hints) covers both today. This bd is for upgrading the automated coverage when bandwidth permits.
design:
## PhotoSwipe (test #3)

Try this trigger strategy (in order of preference):
  a. Hover the parent .product__media element, then wait for .media__zoom__icon to be visible, then click. Validate via headless Playwright before assuming hover-events fire correctly.
  b. If (a) doesn't work: directly invoke the PhotoSwipe init via page.evaluate() targeting the zoom-pswp JS path (look at assets/theme.dev.js productPhotoswipeZoom function for the entry point).
  c. As a last resort: keep the test skipped indefinitely and rely on the Layer 2 manual checklist.

State selector .pswp--open should be correct — verified from theme.dev.css. If state still doesn't appear, try .pswp.pswp--visible or inspect via Chrome DevTools.

## Search overlay (test #4)

Two paths:
  a. RECOMMENDED: Add an `overlay_shape` field to OverlayTest with values like "fullscreen" (default, current assertion) | "popdown" (just checks position:fixed + width >= 300, skips height check) | "modal" (checks centered within viewport). The kt0-killer containment audit still runs in all cases — only the geometry assertion varies.

  b. Simpler: hardcode an `expect_height_pct` field (default 0.9) and set it to 0.05 for popdowns. Less semantic but smaller patch.

The CONTAINMENT_AUDIT_JS stays identical — that's the kt0-killer and shouldn't change between overlay shapes.

## Acceptance after this fix lands

- `python3 scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py --theme=draft` returns 4 PASS, 0 SKIP, 0 FAIL on a clean theme.
- Artificially injecting `contain: layout` onto `.header__wrapper` (or `.product__media`) and re-running causes the corresponding test(s) to FAIL with the kt0-killer reason.
- `.opencode_hints` Layer 3 section can drop the "currently 2 tests work, 2 skipped" caveat.

## Out of scope

- Klaviyo popup, LoyaltyLion launcher, Reamaze chat — these are app-injected, require user state (logged-in customer, etc.), and are well-covered by manual Layer 2 checks. No automated coverage needed.

- `.opencode_hints` Layer 3 section updated if needed to reflect the expanded coverage.
- Single git commit, scoped to scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py + .opencode_hints.

title: Unregister stale Shop Sheriff PWA service workers — 3,585 SW errors / 7d (osj leftover)
description: Shop Sheriff PWA app was uninstalled 2026-05-02 (issue osj). Client-side SW registrations persist in returning shoppers' browsers and fail to update because the upstream serviceworker.js file is gone. JS error pipeline surfaces this as 1,975 (null/nonexistent SW) + 1,610 (failed update) errors / 7d. Bounded fix: small JS in layout/theme.liquid to unregister stale SWs on page load.
design:
## Goal
Unregister stale Shop Sheriff PWA service workers in returning shoppers' browsers. App was uninstalled (issue osj closed 2026-05-02) but the service worker is still registered client-side and failing to update because there's no upstream worker file. ~3,585 events / 7d combined.

## What we know
- Error 1: "Cannot update a null/nonexistent service worker registration" — 1,975 events / 7d
- Error 2: "Failed to update a ServiceWorker for scope ('https://www.hairmnl.com/') with script ('Unknown')" — 1,610 events / 7d
- Source: `(unknown)` (browser-internal SW machinery)
- Cause: Shop Sheriff PWA registered `/serviceworker.js` (or similar). Removing the app uninstalls the upstream file, but client-side SW registration persists.

## Fix (theme-side, mechanical)
Add a small JS snippet to layout/theme.liquid (top of <head> or in a defer'd block) that:
1. Checks if `navigator.serviceWorker` exists.
2. Lists registrations.
3. For each registration whose scriptURL contains 'serviceworker' or 'shopsheriff' or 'pwa', calls `.unregister()`.
4. Logs to console (not GA4 — too noisy) once per session.

Conservative version: only unregister if the registration's `scriptURL` returns 404 (file gone). That way we don't accidentally unregister legitimate SWs from other apps.

## Files to modify
- layout/theme.liquid — add a <script> block, ideally near other one-shot client cleanup logic

## Test plan
1. Push to draft, smoke test in Brave with DevTools → Application → Service Workers visible.
2. If a stale SW is registered, the snippet should remove it within ~1 second of page load.
3. Check console for "[sw-cleanup] unregistered: <url>" or similar log.
4. After live push, watch the dashboard JS errors section over 7 days — both error types should decay to near-zero as users' browsers all get the cleanup script.

## Bounded scope
- ≤30 LoC of JS
- 1 file (layout/theme.liquid)
- No risk if guarded by feature-detection (older browsers without SW support no-op)
acceptance_criteria:
After fix shipped + 7 days of decay:
- "Cannot update null/nonexistent SW" + "Failed to update ServiceWorker" combined < 100 events / 7d (was ~3,585)
- No regression on legitimate SWs (none currently in use)
- Console log shows the cleanup ran once per session for affected users
notes: Closed 2026-05-09 wontfix — user correction: Shop Sheriff PWA is STILL INSTALLED (only Shop Sheriff AMP was uninstalled, issue osj). The SW errors are Shop Sheriff PWA's own code bugs, not theme-side leftovers. Cannot touch — vendor's responsibility. If error volume becomes problematic, route to Shop Sheriff PWA vendor support.

title: BookThatApp gating — 171KB on every mobile pageload
description: cdn.bookthatapp.com loads 171KB on every mobile pageload via SATCB dynamic injection (same mechanism as VQB, confirmed 2026-05-12). Theme-side gating IS possible using the document.createElement guard already installed in layout/theme.liquid (bd 8z9). Task: extend the existing guard to also block cdn.bookthatapp.com on non-booking pages, after discovering which routes legitimately use BookThatApp.
design:
## Context
layout/theme.liquid already has a VQB guard (bd 8z9, merged 2026-05-12, commit cc8d92d) that:
- Overrides document.createElement to swallow script.src assignments to VQB_ORIGINS
- Uses a MutationObserver fallback
- Has a regex allowlist for quiz pages: /\/pages\/[a-z0-9-]*quiz/i

The guard is at roughly line 680 (before {{ content_for_header }}). BookThatApp's loader `bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js` was observed at head script index 40 (async+defer), loaded from `https://www.hairmnl.com/apps/bookthatapp/sdk/v1/js/bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js`. The main bundle comes from `cdn.bookthatapp.com`.

## What OC needs to do

### Step 1 — Discover booking route(s)
Search theme templates and sections for BookThatApp references:
- `grep -rn -i "bookthatapp\|book.that\|bta" templates/ sections/ snippets/ config/`
- Look for any page template JSON or section referencing BTA widgets
- Check if there's a `/pages/book-appointment`, `/pages/appointments`, `/pages/services` or similar

### Step 2 — Extend the guard in layout/theme.liquid
Find the VQB guard block (search for `VQB performance gate` comment or `VQB_ORIGINS`). Extend it:

**Current:**
```js
var VQB_ORIGINS = ['cdn.visualquizbuilder.com'];
var path = window.location.pathname;
var allowed = /\/pages\/[a-z0-9-]*quiz/i.test(path);
```

**Target:**
```js
var BLOCKED_ORIGINS = ['cdn.visualquizbuilder.com', 'cdn.bookthatapp.com'];
var path = window.location.pathname;
var allowed = /\/pages\/[a-z0-9-]*quiz/i.test(path)      // VQB quiz pages
           || /\/(pages|apps)\/[a-z0-9-]*(book|appoint)/i.test(path); // BTA booking pages
```
Also update the `{%- comment -%}` header to reference both bd 8z9 and xex.
Replace `VQB_ORIGINS` with `BLOCKED_ORIGINS` in both the `set` handler and the MutationObserver filter.

**IMPORTANT:** The booking page allowlist regex `/\/(pages|apps)\/[a-z0-9-]*(book|appoint)/i` is a starting guess — adjust based on what Step 1 finds. If BTA lives on a different path pattern, use the real paths.

### Step 3 — Verify JS is syntactically valid
Run: `node --check layout/theme.liquid 2>&1 | grep -A2 "SyntaxError"` (will error on Liquid tags but JS errors show up clearly). Or check braces/parens balance manually.

### Step 4 — Stage only layout/theme.liquid
`git add layout/theme.liquid`
Do NOT commit — coordinator (Claude Code) owns the draft-push smoke-test and live push.

## Hard rules
- Do NOT push to Shopify (no `shopify theme push`)
- Do NOT commit
- Do NOT modify any file other than layout/theme.liquid
- Do NOT change the MutationObserver or createElement override logic — only extend BLOCKED_ORIGINS and the allowlist regex

title: /account page CLS+LCP+FCP+TTFB all in poor — server-side + app-injection issues
description:
Real-shopper RUM data 2026-04-28 shows /account page failing 4 of 5 Core Web Vitals (only INP passes):
- TTFB: 30% good (Shopify customer-area auth-check + customer.orders DB query — server-side, hard to fix from theme)
- FCP: 36% good
- LCP: 44% good (well below 75% CrUX threshold)
- CLS: 35% good
- INP: 96% good ✓

Template code (templates/customers/account.liquid, 118 lines) is clean — just orders table.

Root causes (theme can't fix all, but can mitigate):
1. Shopify customer-area pages aren't CDN-cached (auth required) → slow TTFB inherent
2. LimeSpot / LoyaltyLion / Klaviyo activate fully on logged-in pages → heavy JS
3. PWA modal injection (separate beads — see #pwa-modal CSS fix)
4. customer.orders query renders inline → LCP delays

Theme-side actions to try:
1. Conditionally skip non-essential apps on /account templates (LimeSpot recs, etc — they don't drive customer-area conversion anyway)
2. Minimize CSS loaded on customer-area templates (per-template CSS already split, but customer-area might pull more than needed)
3. Investigate if Klaviyo signup modal fires on /account (often does) — it shouldn't

Not worth a major code investment until other fixes shipped — /account is low-traffic (216 events / 2 days vs homepage's 1,617). But it's the only page with TTFB <50% so worth a dedicated 1-2 hr session.
close_reason: Theme-side mitigation shipped (commit a8407fc): LimeSpot SDK now skipped on customers/* templates — saves ~300KB JS + DNS + parse on every customer-area page request. The remaining TTFB / LCP issues are server-side (Shopify customer-area auth-check, customer.orders DB query) — unfixable from theme code. Other app-skip candidates (VQB, Klaviyo signup modal, Judge.me) noted in commit message for future iteration. Re-open if RUM after 14d shows /account still failing 4+ metrics.

title: Snippet DRY-up: consolidate 3 product-grid-item variants (~600 lines duplicated)
description:
Three nearly-identical files with significant code duplication:
  - snippets/product-grid-item.liquid (standard, used by most collections)
  - snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid (used by branded collection landing pages)
  - snippets/product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid (used by sub-sections of branded collections)

All three render product cards with: WCProduct_Price logic (Wholesale Club), WebP <picture> wrap, brand-prefix strip from title, sticker (sale/sold/new), price + compare-at price, Judge.me badge.

Differences between the three:
  - branded variants strip brand prefix more aggressively (Davines/Aveda/etc whole word matches)
  - branded-subcollection has narrower image sizes and tighter padding
  - standard has hover-second-image swap; branded does not

Proposed consolidation:
1. Rename product-grid-item.liquid → product-card.liquid (new canonical).
2. Add params: variant ('standard'|'branded'|'branded-subcollection'), strip_brand_prefix (bool), show_hover_image (bool), image_sizes (string).
3. Branch internally based on params.
4. Replace all callers — sweep snippets/sections for {% render 'product-grid-item-*' %} and migrate to {% render 'product-card', variant: '...' %}.
5. Keep the old files as { include 'product-card', variant: 'XXX' } for one release cycle, then delete.

Risk: high regression risk on collection / branded-collection pages. Need to test rendering on:
  - /collections/all (standard)
  - /collections/davines (branded)
  - /collections/loreal-professionnel (branded sub-collections)
  - homepage product grids

Worth 3-4 hours: 1 hr to consolidate, 1 hr to migrate callers, 1-2 hr to test all variants. High maintenance value (any future product card change touches 1 file instead of 3) but no shopper-facing impact.

title: Critical CSS audit + regeneration: ~40KB inline contains 37 Swiper + 10 Flickity selectors, some duplicates
description:
2026-04-28 spot audit of snippets/critical-css.liquid (39,927 bytes, single-line minified, inlined in <head> per layout/theme.liquid:17).

Findings:
  - 37 Swiper selectors (.swiper-button-*, .swiper-slide, etc) — used by homepage banner-slider (above-the-fold on homepage). JUSTIFIED.
  - 10 Flickity selectors (.flickity-*) — used by PDP product slideshow (NOT above-the-fold on homepage). Could potentially be moved out of critical CSS for non-PDP pages.
  - At least 5 duplicate rules detected (e.g., .uppercase, .js-grid[data-grid-small=2], 3x .collection__filters__*).
  - Many template-specific rules (.tousled-header__*) that may not be above-the-fold on every template.

Manual pruning is risky — could remove a rule that IS critical on some template. Better approach: regenerate critical CSS via tooling.

Recommended path:
1. Install  npm package (https://github.com/addyosmani/critical) or  (https://github.com/pocketjoso/penthouse).
2. Run against a representative set of templates (homepage, PDP davines-momo, collection davines, /cart, /pages/about, blog article).
3. Diff output vs current critical-css.liquid — identify rules to remove (or keep).
4. Manually merge: union of all per-template critical CSS becomes the new shared critical CSS.
5. Likely savings: 10-30% reduction (~4-12 KB) based on the Flickity + duplicates findings.

Alternative: ship the current critical CSS as-is and instead split into per-template inline blocks (e.g., critical-css-pdp.liquid, critical-css-collection.liquid) so each page only inlines what it needs. More code change but more savings.

Worth ~3-4 hr for proper audit + tooling setup + regeneration.

title: Cart drawer skeleton-state delay: AJAX cart populate takes several seconds
description:
After opening the cart drawer, the skeleton placeholder (data-cart-loading + cart__items--blankstate) stays visible for several seconds before items populate. User reports 'froze for several seconds'.

Suspected culprits (in approximate order of likely impact):
1. /cart.js (or section_id=api-cart) endpoint slow due to app overhead — BSS B2B, Bold Brain, Smart SEO, Judge.me, Elevar all hook into cart rendering
2. AJAX request not fired until drawer opens (could pre-fetch on page load idle)
3. Cart drawer rerendering pulls a Liquid section that re-runs all the cart-related app injections each time

Diagnostic steps:
1. Open DevTools Network tab, click cart icon, measure /cart.js timing
2. Check what section_id the drawer fetches (likely api-cart or api-product-popdown)
3. Profile JS execution after items arrive (skeleton-to-real swap performance)

Quick wins:
- Pre-fetch cart on requestIdleCallback (drawer is then instant when user clicks)
- Cache the rendered cart HTML in sessionStorage; refresh on add/remove
- Strip out app injections from the drawer-specific Liquid section (drawer doesn't need full /cart page hydration)

Worth ~30-60 min investigation slot.

title: Header cart icon: defer-load JS race lets click fall through to /cart link before drawer JS binds
description:
On desktop, clicking the cart icon sometimes navigates to /cart standalone page instead of opening the slide-out drawer. On narrower viewports it works correctly.

Root cause: header cart icons (sections/header.liquid lines 134-140 mobile + 189-204 desktop) have BOTH:
  - href={{ routes.cart_url }} (link to /cart)
  - data-drawer-toggle=drawer-cart (JS hook)

The Drawer.connectToggle() handler in theme.js:1980-1994 does e.preventDefault() to suppress link navigation, but only AFTER theme.js loads. theme.js is loaded with defer (theme.liquid:378), so on a fast cold-cache page, the user can click the cart icon BEFORE the deferred script finishes parsing — link navigation wins.

Worsened by 2026-04-27 perf work: jquery, theme.js, storepickup.js all deferred. Gap between paint and JS-ready can be 200-500ms on a fast Mac.

Fix options:
1. Inline a tiny event-delegation script in <head> that listens at document level for clicks on [data-drawer-toggle] and preventDefault until the real handler binds. Hand off after.
2. Remove the href from the desktop cart icon (loses fallback for JS-disabled users; rare).
3. Add a CSS class to <html> when JS is ready, conditionally hide the link's pointer-events until then (jank).
4. Use JS module preload + inline an early data-drawer-toggle stub.

Option 1 is cheapest and safest. ~10 lines of inline JS in <head>.

description:

They aren't — they're also used by the slide-out cart drawer (snippets/cart-drawer.liquid), which appears on PDP/collection/header/every page.   - cart-page.css (~4KB, lines ~3, 297-465): .cart__template + .template__cart__footer rules. Only used by /cart page. Gate to template == 'cart'.

Saves ~3KB gzipped on non-cart pages and restores the cart-page-only gating for the cart-page-only chunk.

title: use_custom_accent_font theme setting is vestigial — toggle exists but never gates custom_accent_font
description:
2026-04-28 theme settings audit found: settings_schema.json defines a checkbox 'use_custom_accent_font' but no Liquid template references it. The actual font selector 'custom_accent_font' (a select) IS referenced in snippets/css-variables.liquid:89-90 — it's applied unconditionally when set, not gated by the toggle.

Fix options:
1. Remove use_custom_accent_font from settings_schema.json (clears merchant UI).
2. Or wire it up: change css-variables.liquid:89 to: if settings.use_custom_accent_font and settings.custom_accent_font (gated).

Option 2 is more user-friendly to merchants (toggle on/off without losing the saved font choice). ~10 min change. Worth verifying which merchants currently have the toggle in different states before rolling out (might cause a visual change for some).
design:
## Goal
Wire the existing `use_custom_accent_font` toggle (settings_schema.json:312) so it actually gates the application of `custom_accent_font` in `snippets/css-variables.liquid`. Currently the toggle is defined but never referenced — `custom_accent_font` is applied unconditionally at line 89.

## Target file (single)
- `snippets/css-variables.liquid` — change ONE line at line 89.

## What to do
The other custom-font settings (`use_custom_base_font`, `product_use_custom_heading_font`) ALREADY use the toggle-as-gate pattern. The accent variant is the only one that's broken. Match the existing pattern.

**Find** (line 89):
```liquid
  if settings.custom_accent_font
    assign accent_font_family = settings.custom_accent_font
  else
    assign accent_font_family = accent_font.family
  endif
```

**Replace with:**
```liquid
  if settings.use_custom_accent_font
    assign accent_font_family = settings.custom_accent_font
  else
    assign accent_font_family = accent_font.family
  endif
```

Single character change: `settings.custom_accent_font` → `settings.use_custom_accent_font` on the `if` line. The `assign` line keeps the original (uses the font value). Pattern now matches `use_custom_base_font` (line 83) verbatim.

## Behavioral change

| Toggle state | Custom font set | Before | After |
|---|---|---|---|
| ON | yes | applies custom | applies custom (unchanged) |
| ON | no (default) | applies default (because falsy) | applies default (unchanged) |
| OFF | yes | **applies custom** | **applies default** ← regression risk |
| OFF | no | applies default | applies default (unchanged) |

The 3rd row is the only behavioral diff. If the merchant currently has `custom_accent_font` set WITHOUT `use_custom_accent_font` toggled on, they'd lose their custom font.

## Mitigation
The agent should NOT modify `config/settings_schema.json` or `config/settings_data.json` (admin-managed, per `.opencode_hints` rule 4). Coordinator will check current settings_data.json after the change to verify whether the merchant currently has the toggle on. If OFF + font set, coordinator pre-flips the toggle in Shopify admin BEFORE pushing to live (admin task), so the change is no-op visually.

## Invariants to preserve
- `accent_font.family` fallback (when no custom font set) stays exactly as is.
- The `else` branch is unchanged.
- No other lines modified.
- Don't touch the parallel branches for `base_font` or `product_heading_font` — they already work.

## How to verify
After the agent commits + the coordinator pushes to live:
1. Open Shopify admin → Theme settings → Typography.
2. Toggle "Use custom font for subheadings and button text" OFF — confirm the storefront uses the default accent font (not the previously-saved custom font).
3. Toggle ON — confirm the saved `custom_accent_font` value applies.
4. Visual check: PDP product subtitle, button text, blog post subheadings — they should reflect the gate state.

## Out of scope
- Removing the `use_custom_accent_font` setting entirely (Option 1 in the bd description). We're going with Option 2 (wire the toggle) per the description's recommendation.
- Pre-flipping the merchant's setting — that's a coordinator/admin action.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single line in snippets/css-variables.liquid:89 changed from `if settings.custom_accent_font` to `if settings.use_custom_accent_font`.
- The `assign accent_font_family = settings.custom_accent_font` line on line 90 remains unchanged.
- All other lines in the file unchanged.
- Diff scope: ONLY `snippets/css-variables.liquid`. NO changes to settings_schema.json, settings_data.json, or assets/theme.css.
- After live push: Shopify admin Typography toggle controls accent font application (verified by toggling on/off and inspecting storefront).

CSS containment gotcha (kt0 cart-drawer regression 2026-05-11, commits 4e6ecc2 / ff1ef80): contain:layout on .header__wrapper trapped position:fixed cart drawer inside header bounds. Per CSS Containment spec, contain:layout (and contain:paint, transform, filter, perspective, will-change:transform) establishes a containing block for fixed/absolute descendants. ALWAYS audit descendants before adding these to a parent selector. The kt0 author comment claiming 'contain:layout is no-visual-impact' was actively wrong and propagated the misunderstanding. See .opencode_hints 'CSS containment + position gotchas' section + 'Pre-push smoke checklist' for the prevention playbook.
